4Pɔ mɛadzya alɛ, nɔma otumũ mɛ̀ ɔsɛ tɛ kiwui ɛlɛ̀ babhɛtɛ wɔnɔ asɛ ezĩ tɛbaba ana. 5Wɔnɔ petee onu beyi nɛ ɔdadalɛ ɔkɔ nɛ̀ beyi nɛ kiwui mɛ. 6Klisoɔ, nɔtɛkɔ bɔzã asɛ bagɔgɔ̃ gɛ adɔ taɖa, pɔ nɔkɔ bɔzã nɛ awɔ̃ tɛ bɔnyana ishu ovunɔ. 7Ɔmɔsɛ bagɛ apɛ asɛ adɔ taɖa, badɔ tɛlɛ elishi, kɛlɛ bagɛ awɔɛ ebukuɔ tsyɛ awɔ̃ɛ ebuku elishi. 8Pɔ gɛ bɔlɛ kɔ kelizaza soɔ, nɔkɔ bɔnyana ishu vunɔ, tɛ bɔkpɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ asɛ kitelũ ɔɖa akpɛkpɛ ana, tɛ bukumũ kidzo mɛ ɖiɖi ɔkɔ tibubu asɛ kikutu ana. 9Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa egi bu ɔlɔ asɛ tɛ bɔtɔ nɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ esi, pɔ tɛ bɔyɛkɛ tibubu ɔgana Apalete Yesu Kristo abha. 10Etsi akɔ ɔlɔ, sɛ tɛ edzi asɛ budzi nɛ̀ awɔ̃ alo bɔdɔ̃ taɖa tsyɛ elo, bɔba zã ɛkpã plɛni. 11Klisoɔ, nɔkpɛ bagɔlɔ kemi tɛ nɔbhɔ bagɔlɔ owunɔ, asɛ kɛbhɛtɛ gɛ nɔbhɛtɛ ɛlɛ ana.
Ɔshakɔkɔ Gɔgɔɛ
12Bɔlɔ adzya alɛ mɔ, otsiɛ alɛ bubisi denɔ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ ɔsɛ tɛ, nozhinɔ ishu esi ɔkɔ bagɛ awɔa axɔ̃ɛ gagalĩ nɛ wɔnɔɛshi, pɛ bagbanà nɔ nɛ Apalete nɛinye mɛ pɛ behu buluhũ akɔ ɔnɔ. 13Nɔkpɛ ɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ alɛ tɛ nɔdɛmɛ nɔ alɛ piapii nɛ balɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa shu so. Nɔzã ishu yɔyɔ mɛ nɛ bagɔlɔ. 14Bɔlɔ adzya alɛ mɔ, bɔkpalà ɔnɔ sɛ tɛ notseɖe nɔ nɛ̀ bagɛ benu banɔ zhɔzhɔrɛ, nɔkpɛ bagɛ kitu bhui balɛ ohuiɛ kemi, nɔplɛnɔ bagɛ ebotsoɔ, tɛ̀ nɔdɔ fɔ̃ɔ ɔkɔ anɔ ka nyia ka. 15Noɖi kɛlɛ ɔkɔ sɛ ananà agɛ lɔkɔ kibui kpa abuia kɛbhɛ akɔ kibui kpa nɔ, pɔ nɔkpɛ kemi ɔsɛ nɔbhɛtɛ ɛyamɛ gbangbã ɔkɔ bagɔlɔ nɛ aka nyia aka tsyɛ.
16Nɔmɔ samɛ kibe nyia be, 17Nɔzã kɛlɛ abha tɛ nokulu Owusoso Akaa, 18Nɔohu ahɔɛ nɛ ebui ka nyia ka mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ kɛlɛinu Owusoso Akaa tɛpɛpɛɛ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ nɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ.
19Notilĩ Ɔfɔfuĩa nɛifúɔ, 20nɔtɛ lɔkɔ ebuiɖɔlu ebuiɛ wɔ ufù nɔ. 21Nɔlũ awɔ̃ oɖi kɛka nyia ka piapii. Nɔnyana kɛgɛ pɛ̃ɛ ovunɔ. 22Nɔshɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ kibui kpa ka nyia ka shu.
23Owusoso Akaa, yɛahɔɛ ishu yɔyɔ, dalɛ wɔnɔ shu tɛ̀ edzinɔ adalɛ. Tɛ̀ Wɔnɔ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ petee, kɛtɔwuiɛ nɛ̀ ishudzyaa zã kɛpɔ mabu mabu nɛ Apalete Yesu Kristo tɛbabaa shu.
24Anunɔ gɛ ekulu wɔnɔ enu kibui gbã ɖɔɖɔ elete, kɛlɛ ababhɛtɛ ɛlɛ abha.
25Bɔlɔ adzya alɛ mɔ, nokulu Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ ɔlɔ. 26Nɔsɛnɔ baadzya petee nɛ̀ ahɔɛ kpɛkpɛ dadalɛ nɛ balɛ olí. 27Ɛlɔkɔ ɛlɛ kpɛ̀ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ nɛ Apaleteɛ inye mɛ sɛ̀ tɛ̀ nɔkalà keplukpa alɛ ɔkɔ badzya petee.
28Apalete Yesu Kristo tibhui bhuibuɔ zã plɛnɔ wɔnɔ.

english_text:
1 From Paul, Silas, and Timothy.
To the church in Thessalonica, the people of God our Father and of the Lord Jesus Christ.
2I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
When Christ Returns
3My dear friends, we always have good reason to thank God for you, because your faith in God and your love for each other keep growing all the time. 4This is why we brag about you to all of God's churches. We tell them how patient you are and how you keep on having faith, even though you are going through a lot of trouble and suffering.
5All this shows that God judges fairly and is making you fit to share in his kingdom for which you are suffering. 6It is only right for God to punish everyone who is causing you trouble, 7but he will give you relief from your troubles. God will do the same for us, when the Lord Jesus comes from heaven with his powerful angels 8and with a flaming fire.
Our Lord Jesus will punish anyone who doesn't know God and won't obey his message. 9 Their punishment will be eternal destruction, and they will be kept far from the presence of our Lord and his glorious strength. 10This will happen on the day when the Lord returns to be praised and honored by all who have faith in him and belong to him. This includes you, because you believed what we said.
11God chose you, and we keep praying that God will make you worthy of being his people. We pray for God's power to help you do all the good things you hope to do and your faith makes you want to do. 12Then, because of the undeserved grace of God and our Lord Jesus Christ, you will bring honor to the name of our Lord Jesus, and he will bring honor to you.
translation_text:
1Paulo, Silvano Nɛ̀ Timoteo, bɔlɔ sɛsɛ ɔhaa nɛ Tesalonika gɛ akpasɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛenu bɔlɔ Akaa nɛ̀ Apalete Yesu Kristo mɛ keplukpa alɛ,
2Tibhui bhuibu nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ odenɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo ɔkɔ.
Ahɔɛhuhu Nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa Tikukulu
3Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ buhu Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ kibe nyia be ɔkɔ wɔnɔ, kɛpɛ ana, ɔmɔsɛ gɛ wɔnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã kpe tɔɖɔ soɔ, tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ gɛ kpasɛ wɔnɔ mɛ ɔkɔ bagɔlɔ tsyɛ kpe tɔɖɔ. 4Klisoɔ, nɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔhaa nɛishiɛ, butũ ahɔɛ nɛitélũ nɛ kana gɛ nɔkpɛ kemi nɛ wɔnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã, kede tɛtɔtɔnɔ nɛ̀ tifefekeɖiɛ gɛ nɔkpɛ emi ɔkɔ.
5Alɛ pete ebu akasɛ asɛ Owusoso Akaa kebhu kukuluɔ alɛ kalɛ ɔzãkɔ, kɛlɛ nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shu soɔ, babà kalà wɔnɔ asɛ banɔ gɛ ɔna ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa bukusi ɔŋãkɔɔ, gɛ bɔlɛ shu nɔmɔ bɔtɔ ɔlɛ. 6Owusoso Akaa atɛasɛ asɛnɛ, aba buia bɔtɔ ɛbhɛ akɔ bagɛ bakpɛ wɔnɔ buluhũɔ 7kɛlɛ abakɔ wɔnɔ bagɛ bakpɛ buluhũɔ emoelè, kana bɔlɔ tsyɛ. Kibui ɛlɛ babà ɛlɛ mɛ gɛ onupɛ Apalete Yesu ekoeyì denɔ kititíɛ nɛ kifu gɛtã ɔplɛnɔ yɛ ohumi axɔ̃ɛdɛ. 8Abapɔ̃ batɔ̃ akɔ bagɛ betinyi Owusoso Akaa pɛ batɛ abhɛtɛ atɔɖɔ kibui gbangbã gɛide Apalete Yesu ɔkɔ nabha. 9Babà pɔ̃ atɔ akɔ alɛ nɛ̀ butsi tihɔɔ kɛlɛ abàdã kidzo akpɛ alɛ nɛ Apaleteɛ nɛ̀ yɛ bukusiŋaŋã ohumi namɛ 10nɛ kiwui ɛlɛ nabha gɛ ababaa sɛ tɛ badalɛ yɛishu nɛ yɛ banɔ dadalɛ nɛishiɛ, nɛ̀ tɛ yɛibui bhɛtɛ bagɛ ayɛkɛ yɛabha ŋã pete nɛibì. Wɔnɔ tsyɛ ɔkpasɛ ɛlɛ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ nɔyɛkɛ kibuiɛ gɛ bɔmɔlú pɛ butseɖe wɔnɔ nabha ɔŋã.
11Gɛ kibui ɛlɛ kpasɛ bɔlɔ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ mɛ soɔ, bukulu Owusoso Akaa nɛ wɔnɔ ɛtakpu kibè nyia bè, sɛ tɛ̀ bɔlɔ Owusoso Akaa ayɛkɛ wɔnɔ asɛ nɔna ɔkɔ yɛ tikukuluɔ, kɛlɛ nɛ yɛ ohumi nulũɔ, ababhɛtɛ tɔɖɔ wɔnɔ ebui gbangbã tɔtɔɖɔ nɛ̀ katsɛɖɛ bubu ka nyia ka gɛ wɔnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã kɔ nobuɔ nabha. 12Buukulù Owusoso Akaa nɛ kibui ɛlɛ ɛtakpu sɛ tɛ̀ badalɛ Apaleteɛ Yesu kinye nɛ wɔnɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ wɔnɔ tsyɛ nɛ yɛ mɛ, nɛ kibhuiɛ gɛ bɔlɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Apalete Yesu Kristo ebhui bɔlɔ buɔ nulũ.

english_text:
The Lord's Return
1 When our Lord Jesus returns, we will be gathered up to meet him. So I ask you, my friends, 2not to be easily upset or disturbed by people who claim the Lord has already come. They may say they heard this directly from the Holy Spirit, or from someone else, or even that they read it in one of our letters. 3But don't be fooled! People will rebel against God. Then before the Lord returns, the wicked one who is doomed to be destroyed will appear. 4 He will brag and oppose everything holy or sacred. He will even sit in God's temple and claim to be God. 5Don't you remember I told you this while I was still with you?
6You already know what is holding this wicked one back until it is time for him to come. 7His mysterious power is already at work, but someone is holding him back. And the wicked one won't appear until this someone is out of the way. 8 Then he will appear, but the Lord Jesus will kill him simply by breathing on him. He will be completely destroyed by the Lord's glorious return.
9 When the wicked one appears, Satan will pretend to work all kinds of miracles, wonders, and signs. 10Lost people will be fooled by his evil deeds. They could be saved, but they will refuse to love the truth and accept it. 11So God will make sure they are fooled into believing a lie. 12All of them will be punished, because they would rather do evil than believe the truth.
Be Faithful
13My friends, the Lord loves you, and it is only natural for us to thank God for you. God chose you to be the first ones to be saved. His Spirit made you holy, and you put your faith in the truth. 14God used our preaching as his way of inviting you to share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ. 15My friends, this is why you must remain faithful and follow closely what we taught you in person and by our letters.
16God our Father loves us. He treats us with undeserved grace and has given us eternal comfort and a wonderful hope. We pray that our Lord Jesus Christ and God our Father 17will encourage you and help you always to do and say the right thing.
translation_text:
Anɔ Gɛenu Kɛtakpumɛ Gagalĩ Eleteɛ
1Nɛ Apalete Yesu tɛbaba nɛ̀ bɔlɔ tishoshogò nɛ̀ yɛishuɔ, bubisi wɔnɔ, badzya, 2sɛ tɛ nɔtɛkɔ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ kibui ɖɔlù, bulũ bhui bhui, alo keplukpa tieli gɛ basɛ kede bɔlɔkɔ pɛ kaɖɔ sɛ Apaleteɛ nɛiwuiɛ tɛba kukuku. 3Nɔtɛkɔ anɔ nanà awɔɛ wɔnɔ ɔganà kidzo nanà abha, ɔmɔsɛ flogo tɛ kiwui ɛlɛ baà, kɛlɛ sɛ kititì lɔlɔ putɔ, kɛlɛ bebebu Satana akasɛ, anunɔ gɛ batɔɖɔ butsi elu akɔ. 4Anɔ alɛ aba lɔɛtití kɛlɛ ebe feke yɛishu nɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛ bekulu sɛ Owusoso Akaa alo pɛ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ shu, sɛ tɛ edidzu yɛishu nɛ Owusoso Akaa uvũɔ mɛ asɛ yɛenu Owusoso Akaa.
5Nɔgɛ baka sɛ gɛ ɛzã wɔnɔ ɔbhaa, izitseɖe wɔnɔ ebui alɛ dza? 6Kɛlɛ otsi alɛ nɔonyi kɛgɛ kɔ yɛawɔ ideɛ, sɛ tɛ bebebu yɛakasɛ nɛ kibè gbangbã nabha. 7Ɔmɔsɛ Satana tikpukpũ mɛ ohumi nɔ gɛonu ɔsha abha mabhɛtɛ mabhɛtɛ lɔtɛ kpɛ ɔlɛ axɔ̃ɛ esi, pɔ anunɔ gɛedzu ahɔɛ nɛ yɛabha abazã ɛlɛ bha vɛnɔ odiyinɔ sɛ bebu yɛeyinɔ nɛidzoɔ ɛlɛ nabha. 8Kɛlɛ bebèbu kɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩ eleteɛ akasɛ, anunɔ gɛ Apalete Yesu abalɔkɔ yɛolugbũ mɛ ɛlɛɛ́ nɔ atsɛ yɛ atɛɛ niegbu nabha kɛlɛ abalɔkɔ yɛ titsetséɛ nɛ yɛ tɛbaba mɛ eyii nɔ. 9Kɛtakpumɛ gagalĩ eleteɛ tɛbaba bashɔnɔ Satana axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa gɛ bebu akasɛ nɛ asɛnɛ atsyindzye ebui, bɔdɔ̃ bubu kasɛ nɛ̀ ebui gɛ abhɛtɛ ibi mɛ, 10nɛ ebui kpa ka nyia ka ɛdi gɛ lawɔɛ bagɛ ayalaa. Bayala ɔmɔsɛ bagɛ dɛmɛ kibuiɛ gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ nɛibui sɛ tɛ bebu alɛ. 11Kibui ɛlɛ shu soɔ Owusoso Akaa asɛsɛ alɛ asɛnɛ ohumi sɛ tɛ bayɛkɛ asɛnɛ nabha ŋã 12sɛ tɛ bebù bagɛ agɛ yɛkɛ kibui gbã nabha ŋã nɛpɔ, pɛ kɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ eku alɛ same.
Nɔnyanà Ebuiɛ Mɛ Ovunɔ Gagalĩi
13Pɔ badzya gɛ Apaleteɛ adɛmɛ nɔ, kɛlɛ sɛ buzi hu Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ kiwui nyia wui nɛ wɔnɔ ɛtakpu, ɔmɔsɛ denɔ kesikpɛkpɛ mɛibeɛ, Owusoso Akaa ebu wɔnɔ elú ɔgana ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ nɛ̀ ɔlɛ axɔ̃ɛ dadalɛ wɔawɔa mɛ, nɛ̀ ɔganà kibui gbã tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ. 14Ekulu wɔnɔ akpɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ ɛlɛ mɛ ɔgana kibui gbangbã mɛ, sɛ tɛ nɔmɔ tɛdadalɛ gɛ kpasɛ ɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristoɔ mɛ. 15Klisoɔ, mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, nɔzã gagalĩi nɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ gɛ bɔlɔkɔ kpɛ wɔnɔ ahɔɛ mɛ, onupɛ kizidzì olugbũ mɛibui alo keplukpa fɔfɔnɔ mɛ tsyɛ. 16Tɛ bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristoɔ, nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa, anɔ gɛ adɛmɛ nɛ ɔlɔ pɛ nɛ yɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ mɛɛ, akɔ ɔlɔ kemikpɛkpɛ tihɔɔ ɔkɔ kidzo gbangbã mɛ tiɖiɖiɛ, 17abakpɛ wɔnɔ kitúɔ niemi pɛ abakɔ wɔnɔ ohumi nɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gbangbã ka nyia ka wɔawɔa nɛ kibuiɛ mɛ.

english_text:
Pray for Us
1Finally, our friends, please pray for us. This will help the message about the Lord to spread quickly, and others will respect it, just as you do. 2Pray that we may be kept safe from worthless and evil people. After all, not everyone has faith. 3But the Lord can be trusted to make you strong and protect you from harm. 4He has made us sure that you are obeying what we taught you and that you will keep on obeying. 5I pray that the Lord will guide you to be as loving as God and as patient as Christ.
Warnings against Laziness
6My dear friends, in the name of the Lord Jesus, I beg you not to have anything to do with any of your people who loaf around and refuse to obey the instructions we gave you. 7You surely know that you should follow our example. We didn't waste our time loafing, 8and we didn't accept food from anyone without paying for it. We didn't want to be a burden to any of you, so night and day we worked as hard as we could.
9We had the right not to work, but we wanted to set an example for you. 10We also gave you this rule: If you don't work, you don't eat. 11Now we learn that some of you just loaf around and won't do any work, except the work of a busybody. 12So, for the sake of our Lord Jesus Christ, we ask and beg these people to settle down and start working for a living. 13Dear friends, you must never become tired of doing right.
14Be on your guard against any followers who refuse to obey what we have written in this letter. Put them to shame by not having anything to do with them. 15Don't consider them your enemies. Instead, speak kindly to them as you would to any other follower.
Final Prayer
16I pray that the Lord, who gives peace, will always bless you with peace. May the Lord be with all of you.
17I always sign my letters as I am now doing: PAUL.
18I pray that our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to all of you.
translation_text:
Owusoso Akaa Tikukulu Tibibisi
1Gɔgɔɛ nɔ, mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, nokulu Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ ɔlɔ, sɛ tɛ Apaleteɛ nɛibuiɛ yì falanɔ nɛ tɛtsɛɖɛ bubu mɛ tɛ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ ɛlɛ, asɛ kɛlɛ gɛ kɛlɛ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ ana. 2Klisoɔ nokulu Owusoso Akaa sɛ tɛ bebu ɔlɔ odenɔ atakpu mɛ gagalĩ elete nɛ̀ banɔ zizĩ ahɔɛ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ gɛnɛ aka nyia aka yayɛkɛ aŋã. 3Pɔ Apaleteɛ atɛasɛ asɛnɛ, abakpɛ wɔnɔ ohumi kɛlɛ ebebu wɔnɔ odenɔ anɔ kpa ahɔɛ mɛ. 4Bɔyɛkɛ Apaleteɛ nabha ɔŋã sɛ notsiɖì ɛlɛ abha pɛ nɔbhɛtɛ, kɛlɛ nɔbɔ zã ɛlɛ abha ɔzɔbhɛtɛ ɔtɔɖɔ ɔsha gɛ bɔkpɛ kɔ wɔnɔ abha. 5Nɔkɔ Apaleteɛ ashɔrɔ wɔnɔ etúɔ akpɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa nɛ kemi kpɛkpɛ nɛ Kristo mɛ.
Titsetseɖe Nɔ Nɛ Kɛxɔ̃ɛ Mawɔa Mawɔa Shu
6Mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, nɛ Apalete Yesu Kristo kinyeɛ mɛ, bɔɔkpɛ wɔnɔ ɔsha, sɛ nɔtɛ kpɛ ɔha nɛ adzya ka nyia ka gɛ edzi nɛ ahɔɛkpakpa pɛ atɛa zana ɛkpã nɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ gɛ nɔyɛkɛ odenɔ bɔlɔ ahɔɛ mɛ shu. 7Ɔmɔsɛ nɔonyi katsɛɖɛ gɛ kɛlɛ sɛ nobu tɛ nɔgã nɛ bɔlɔ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ nulũ. Bɔgɛ zã kɛxɔ̃ɛ mawɔa mawɔa nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bɔzã wɔnɔ ɔbha, 8alo pɛ bɔŋã anɔ teli bɔdɔ̃ gɛ bɔgɛ buia bɔlɛ ɛbhɛ. Kibui ɛlɛ shu soɔ, bɔwɔa axɔ̃ɛ elishi nɛ̀ kemuze, kɛlɛ buuwu pɛ buuzhì nɛ bɔtɔ mɛ sɛ tɛ bɔtɛ badzi bɔtɔ ɔkɔ wɔnɔ mɛ anɔ nanà. 9Kibui ɛlɛ gɛ bɔbhɛtɛ, kitinu sɛ gɛ bɔgɛ shɔnɔ sɛ bɔyɛkɛ tɛpɛplɛ nɔ ɛlɛ nɛidi denɔ wɔnɔ ɔkɔ so, pɔ sɛ tɛ bɔbhɛtɛ kasɛ wɔnɔ tɛ nɔgã nɛ ɛlɛ shu. 10Gɛ bɔzã wɔnɔ ɔbha tsyɛ elo, bɔkɔ wɔnɔ ɔsha ɔlɛ sɛ, “Onupɛ anɔ atɛba wɔa kɛxɔ̃ɛ, atɛŋã bɔdɔ̃ tsyɛ.”
11Bulũ sɛ banɔ teli nɛ wɔnɔɛishi bedzi nɛ ahɔɛ kpakpa. Batɛ adɛmɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa ɛbui, pɔ olugbũ kedzi ete bagã. 12Banɔ alɛ nɛidi bɔkɔ ɔsha pɛ bɔkpalà alɛ nɛ Apalete Yesu mɛ sɛ bapɛ ɔkɔ toli azã tɛ bawɔa kɛxɔ̃ɛ tɛ baŋã ɔdɔ̃. 13Pɔ mɛadzya alɛ mɔ, wɔnɔ xɔnɔ, nɔtɛ mɔ ɔtɔ odenɔ kibui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ.
14Onupɛ anɔ teli agɛ bhɛtɛ atɔɖɔ bɔlɔ ɔsha kpɛkpɛ ɔlɛ nabha nɛ keplukpa alɛ mɛɛ, nɔolú awɔ̃ oɖi piapii. Nɔtɔ kpɛ yɛɔha, sɛ tɛ kiwũi yii. 15Pɔ nɔtɛ lɔkɔ yɛasɛ anɔ gɛ nɔtɔɖɔ olú ana, pɔ notseɖe nii asɛ adzya ana.
Tɛsɛsɛnɔ Piapii Tili Idi
16Nɔkɔ ishu yɔyɔ Apaleteɛ, akɔ wɔnɔ ishu yɔyɔ kibe nyia be nɛidzo ka nyia ka abha. Apaleteɛ azã aplɛnɔ wɔnɔ petee.
17Mɛ, Paulo mɛfɔnɔ ɔsɛsɛnɔ ɔlɛ nɛ̀ mɛahɔɛ, gɛ kenu mɛeplukpa gɛ kagã ɔtɔ nɛ mɛ buplukpa petee nɛishi. Tɛ̀ nɔmɔ ɔsɛ mɛahɔɛ bɔdɔ̃ fɔfɔnɔ nɔlɛ.
18Tɛ̀ bɔlɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo tibhui bhuibuɔ zã ɔplɛnɔ wɔnɔ petee.

english_text:
1From Paul.
God our Savior and Christ Jesus commanded me to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, who gives us hope.
2 Timothy, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind and merciful to you. May they bless you with peace!
Warning against False Teaching
3When I was leaving for Macedonia, I asked you to stay on in Ephesus and warn certain people there to stop spreading their false teachings. 4You needed to warn them to stop wasting their time on senseless stories and endless lists of ancestors. Such things only cause arguments. They don't help anyone to do God's work that can only be done by faith.
5You must teach people to have genuine love, as well as a good conscience and true faith. 6There are some who have given up these for nothing but empty talk. 7They want to be teachers of the Law of Moses. But they don't know what they are talking about, even though they think they do.
8We know the Law is good, if it is used in the right way. 9We also understand it wasn't given to control people who please God, but to control lawbreakers, criminals, godless people, and sinners. It is for wicked and evil people, and for murderers, who would even kill their own parents. 10The Law was written for people who are sexual perverts or who live as homosexuals or are kidnappers or liars or won't tell the truth in court. It is for anything else that opposes the correct teaching 11of the good news the glorious and wonderful God has given me.
Being Thankful for God's Kindness
12I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me the strength for my work because he knew he could trust me. 13 I used to say terrible and insulting things about him, and I was cruel. But Christ had mercy on me because I didn't know what I was doing, and I had not yet put my faith in him. 14Christ Jesus our Lord treated me with undeserved grace and has greatly blessed my life with faith and love just like his own.
15“Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners.” This saying is true, and it can be trusted. I was the worst sinner of all! 16But since I was worse than anyone else, God had mercy on me and let me be an example of the endless patience of Christ Jesus. He did this so that others would put their faith in Christ and have eternal life. 17I pray that honor and glory will always be given to the only God, who lives forever and is the invisible and eternal King! Amen.
18Timothy, my son, the instructions I am giving you are based on what some prophets once said about you. If you follow these instructions, you will fight like a good soldier. 19You will be faithful and have a clear conscience. Some people have made a mess of their faith because they didn't listen to their consciences. 20Two of them are Hymenaeus and Alexander. I have given these men over to the power of Satan, so they will learn not to oppose God.
translation_text:
1Mɛ Paulo gidzi, Yesu Kristo yɛanɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa gɛebu ɔlɔ yɛɔsha kɔkɔ nulũɔ nɛ̀ Yesu Kristoɔ gɛenu anɔ gɛ buɖi kidzo mɛ ɔkɔ,
2Mɛ sɛsɛ Timoteo mɛeyianyɛ gɛ akpasɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ keplukpa alɛ,
Tibhui bhuibu, ashagabui ɖiɖi nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ de Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Kristo Yesu bɔlɔ Apaleteɛ ɔkɔ.
Titsetseɖe Nɔ Nɛ Asɛnɛ Ɔshadɔkasɛ Shu
3Asɛ kana gɛ ɛkpalà wɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ ɛvɛ Makedonia nɔ, zã Efesoɔ tɛ̀ ɔkpɛ ɔsha ɔkɔ banɔ nɔ teli, sɛ batɛ zakasɛ asɛnɛ bɔdɔ̃ 4alo tɛ̀ balɔkɔ ishu ashɔkpɛ asɛnɛ ebui nɛ̀ eshopo tɛshɔshɔrɔ gɛeti beleɛ mɛ. Ebui alɛ amana kawlɔ̃akɛ ebui ɔdɛ Owusoso Akaa kɛxɔ̃ɛ-gɛinu tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã. 5Kɛgɛ kɛlɛishu bakpɛ ɔsha ɔlɛ kɛlɛinu tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ, gɛide kitúɔ gɛ dalɛ nɛ̀ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ gɛ yɛmɛ adalɛ ɔplɛnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã gbangbã. 6Beteli ashɛ nɛ ebui alɛ ede pɛ betsyina avɛ kaɖɔɖɔɖɔ gɛ kesi lulũ nanà ama kalɛishuɔ mɛ. 7Bapɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bedzi ɔshadɔkasɛ, pɔ betinyi kɛnɔnɔ shu gɛ baɖɔaɖɔ alo kɛnɔnɔ abha gɛ batɔɖɔ kɛklagbuɔ.
8Bunyi sɛ ɔsha pɛ gɛ onupɛ bawɔa ɔlɛ shu axɔ̃ɛ piapii. 9Bunyi tsyɛ sɛ, bagɛ bhɛtɛ ɔsha akɔ banɔ dadalɛ pɔ babhɛtɛ ɔlɛ akɔ bagɛ betso ɔsha nɛ̀ batɔ̃mɛ gagalĩ eleteɛ. Bagɛ betinyi Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ eleteɛ, bagɛ balɛishu gɛdalɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ batɛ akpɛ ketakpu esiɛ, lɔlɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ eyí balɛ akaalɛ alo balɛ elĩ alɛ, lɔlɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ eyi anɔ, 10lɔlɛ ɔkɔ ɔmamɛ dɛdɛ elete, nɛ̀ bagɛ abhɛtɛ atsɔ̃buiɛ, lɔlɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ agã banɔ dzizì edziɛ, basɛnɛ elete nɛ̀ bagɛ betielũ atɔ̃ akɔ ɔsha-nɛ̀ kɛka nyia ka ɛdi gɛ kɛgã ɔtɔ nɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ gbangbã shuɔ 11ɔsha gɛ lɔbhɛtɛ tenikpo nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa gɛ beyulà yɛibui gbangbã, kibui gɛ alɔkɔ akpɛ mahɔɛ mɛ.
Kibhuiɛ Gɛ Apaleteɛ Ebhui Paulo Ebu
12Ihu bɔlɔ Apalete Kristo Yesu ahɔɛ, anɔ gɛ yɛakɔ mɛ ohumi, pɛ ayɛkɛ mɛabha aŋã sɛ ɛtɛsɛ asɛnɛ, pɛ ebu mɛ akɔ yɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa. 13Gɛ itidzì anɔ gɛ ɛbhɛtɛ ebusoebui eɖi, anɔ gɛ atɔnɔ banɔ ede nɛ̀ anɔ gɛ azabhɛtɛ agbagbà ebui tsyɛ elo, ebi mɛ ashagabui, ɔmɔsɛ ɛbhɛtɛ alɛ petee nɛ matì matì nɛ̀ mayɛkɛ mayɛkɛŋã mɛ. 14Apaleteɛ akɔ mɛ yɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ nɛ tikpekpe mɛ, ɔlɔkɔ plɛnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ̀ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ gɛ kɛkpasɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ.
15Kɛlɛ kɛlɛinu kibui gbã tɛɖɔɖɔ gɛ kɛlɛ sɛ bayɛkɛ ɛlɛ sɛ, Kristo Yesu aba kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ tɛ ebu ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ eleteɛ, gɛ nɛ balɛishiɛ, mɛinu ezizĩɛ ɔdɛ. 16Pɔ nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shu so pɛ beɖi mɛ ashagabui, sɛ tɛ nɛ mɛtakpuɔ, anɔ gɛezĩ adɛ nɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ elete nɛishi, tɛ̀ Kristo Yesu ebu yɛitu esi luluɔ gɛ ɛlɛ ɔtɔ̃kɔ ma akasɛ. Sɛ tɛ̀ bagɛ aba yɛkɛ yɛabha aŋã amɔ ɛkpã tihɔɔ. 17Otsi nɔ, tɛ̀ Ekusiɛ gɛ abazã tihɔɔ nɔ, anɔ gɛ etibetsì eɖi, anɔ gɛ batɛamɔ yɛ, Owusoso Akaa gɛ yɛ ete, yɛ kɛlɛ sɛ balɔkɔ kɛtakpu esi kpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ ishu dadalɛ akɔ ɔvɛnɔ tihɔɔ mɛ. Amen.
18Mɛeyianyɛ Timoteo, ɛkɔ wɔ ɔsha ɔlɛ sɛ tɛ ebuiɛ gɛ ebuiɖɔluanɔ aɖɔ nɛ wɔshuɔ aba alɛ mɛ, sɛ tɛ onupɛ ɔzã alɛ edeɛ, obodzi axɔ̃ɛ gbangbã wɔawɔa elete, 19tɛ ɔnyana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ ovunɔ nɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ gɛ adalɛ mɛ. Beteli agɛ yɛkɛ ebui alɛ abha aŋã, klisoɔ balɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã tsí. 20Balɛ mɛ beteli balɛenu Himenaio nɛ̀ Aleksandro, banɔ gɛ ɛlɔkɔ alɛ kpɛ ahɔɛ ɔkɔ Satana sɛ akasɛ alɛ ɔdɔ̃ asɛ batɛɖɔ ebusoebui.

english_text:
How To Pray
1First of all, I ask you to pray for everyone. Ask God to help and bless them all, and tell God how thankful you are for each of them. 2Pray for kings and others in power, so we may live quiet and peaceful lives as we worship and honor God. 3This kind of prayer is good, and it pleases God our Savior. 4God wants everyone to be saved and to know the whole truth, which is,
5There is only one God,
and Christ Jesus
is the only one
who can bring us
to God.
Jesus was truly human,
and he gave himself
to rescue all of us.
6God showed us this
at the right time.
7 This is why God chose me to be a preacher and an apostle of the good news. I am telling the truth. I am not lying. God sent me to teach the Gentiles about faith and truth.
8I want everyone everywhere to lift innocent hands toward heaven and pray, without being angry or arguing with each other.
9 I would like for women to wear modest and sensible clothes. They should not have fancy hairdos, or wear expensive clothes, or put on jewelry made of gold or pearls. 10Women who claim to love God should do helpful things for others, 11and they should learn by being quiet and paying attention. 12They should be silent and not be allowed to teach or to tell men what to do. 13 After all, Adam was created before Eve, 14 and the man Adam wasn't the one who was fooled. It was the woman Eve who was completely fooled and sinned. 15But women will be saved by having children, if they stay faithful, loving, holy, and modest.
translation_text:
Bɔdɔ̃ Kakasɛ Nɛ Kɛtakpu Esi Kpɛkpɛ Nɛ Ɔhaa Mɛ
1Putɔ nɔɔ, ɛkpalà wɔ ɔsɛ, kɛdɔ̃ bibisi, Owusoso Akaa tikukulu, pɛnɔsɛ tɛ̀ bekulu Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ anɔ teli alo tɛ behu ahɔɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ babhɛtɛ alɛ akɔ anɔ ka nyia ka, 2akɔ bekusiɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ alɛ ɔsha nulũɔ, sɛ tɛ bɔzã nɛ ishu yɔyɔ nɛ̀ ɛkpã gɛyɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ ishu dadalɛ pete mɛ. 3Kibui ɛlɛ pɛ̃, gɛ kiku Owusoso Akaa gɛ ebu ɔlɔ awɔ̃, 4anɔ gɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ bebu anɔ ka nyia ka sɛ tɛ baba kibui titìɛ gɛ lɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ mɛ. 5Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa tenikpo yɛalɛ, nɛ̀ anɔ tenikpo gɛevũ iboe nɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ banɔdzyrame ishi, gɛenu Kristo Yesu, 6anɔ gɛ alɔkɔ yɛ ɛkpã akɔ nɛ banunɔ petee takpu-kibui gɛ badalɛ kɛlɛishu nɛ kɛlɛ ibè gbangbã mɛ. 7Nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shu soɔ, bebu mɛ asɛ anɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ adɔkasɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ etinu Yudanɛ, tɛ ɛdɛ ɖɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ibuiɛ gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ ɔkɔ alɛ. Kibui gbã mɛɖɔ, gɛnɛ asɛnɛ mɛsɛ.
8Ɛpɛ sɛ banɔ gɛede ɔkɔ nyia ɔkɔ efeke balɛ ahɔɛ dadalɛ nɛitití ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu, tɛ̀ ɔmɔpɔɛ alo kibui wɔawlɔãkɛ tɛzã ɛlɛ mɛ.
9Ɛpɛ tsyɛ sɛ tɛ badzɛ akpɛ bɔdɔ̃ asɛ badzɛ ana, tɛ̀ apɔɔɖi, ɔkɔ ɔhaɛshi tɛzazã asɛ baadzɛ ana, gɛnɛ tɛ beyu ɛtakpu alo tɛ bazã sikã ɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ gɛ yɛkɛ kedziɛ mɛ, 10pɔ nɛ̀ ebui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ gɛ ashɔnɔ badzɛ gɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ̀ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa.
11Kɛlɛ sɛ anɔdzɛ akpasɛ bɔdɔ̃ nɛ kaɖɔ maɖɔ maɖɔ nɛ̀ ishu esi zhizhinɔ mɛ. 12Ɛgɛ kɔ idzo sɛ anɔdzɛ akasɛ ɔdɔ̃ alo tɛ̀ akɔ anɔnyɛ ɔsha, kɛlɛ sɛ ataatɔ̃. 13Ɔmɔsɛ Adam beputɔ abhɛtɛ, flogo pɛ babhɛtɛ Xawa. 14Kɛlɛ gɛnɛ Adam bawɔɛ, adzɛ bawɔɛ pɛ ebedzì ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ elete. 15Pɔ bebèbu badzɛ ɔgana eyimɔmɔ mɛ, onupɛ bazã ɛlɛ abha nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ, tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ, ishu dadalɛ nɛ̀ ɛkpã zazãna gbangbã mɛ.

english_text:
Church Officials
1It is true that anyone who desires to be a church official wants to be something worthwhile. 2 That's why officials must have a good reputation and be faithful in marriage. They must be self-controlled, sensible, well-behaved, friendly to strangers, and able to teach. 3They must not be heavy drinkers or troublemakers. Instead, they must be kind and gentle and not love money.
4Church officials must be in control of their own families, and they must see that their children are obedient and always respectful. 5If they don't know how to control their own families, how can they look after God's people?
6They must not be new followers of the Lord. If they are, they might become proud and be doomed along with the devil. 7Finally, they must be well-respected by people who are not followers. Then they won't be trapped and disgraced by the devil.
Church Officers
8Church officers should be serious. They must not be liars, heavy drinkers, or greedy for money. 9And they must have a clear conscience and hold firmly to what God has shown us about our faith. 10They must first prove themselves. Then if no one has anything against them, they can serve as officers.
11Women must also be serious. They must not gossip or be heavy drinkers, and they must be faithful in everything they do.
12Church officers must be faithful in marriage. They must be in full control of their children and everyone else in their home. 13Those who serve well as officers will earn a good reputation and will be highly respected for their faith in Christ Jesus.
The Mystery of Our Religion
14I hope to visit you soon. But I am writing these instructions, 15so if I am delayed, you will know how everyone who belongs to God's family ought to behave. After all, the church of the living God is the strong foundation of truth.
16Here is the great mystery of our religion:
Christ came as a human.
The Spirit proved
that he pleased God,
and he was seen by angels.
Christ was preached
to the nations.
People in this world
put their faith in him,
and he was taken up to glory.
translation_text:
Ɛsofo Nɔ Nɛ̀ Ɛhamɛ Ɛzãkɔ Bhlui Eleteɛ
1Kɛlɛ nu tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ibui gɛɖɔ ɔsɛ, Onupɛ anɔ teli atɔɖɔ edenɔ yɛitúɔ mɛ ɔsɛ tɛ edzì osofo, ebu kɛxɔ̃ɛ gɛiku awɔ̃. 2Kɛma ɔsɛ tɛ̀ bazã amɔ bamɛ tsitsí ebui nɛ osofo nɔ shu, edzì anɔ gɛ agbana adzɛ tenikpo, anɔ gɛ elu kitú esi, pɛ aŋã yɛishu abha, anɔ gɛ atɛkpɛ yɛishu awũi, anɔ gɛ adɛmɛ beefu tɛyɛyɛkɛ ibui pɛ alà kasɛ ɔdɔ̃ tsyɛ, 3anɔ gɛ agɛ lɔkɔ yɛishu akɔ bunù gbagbla tɛwɔɛwɔɛ, etidzi agbagbà ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ elete pɔ edzì anɔ yɔyɔ, etidzi ebela pɛelete, pɛ adzã kiwuhũ nɛ ehoeyi shu. 4Kɛlɛ ɔsɛ edzi anɔ gɛeɖi yɛɔpã abha piapii, tɛ̀ eɖi ɛlɛ ɔkɔ asɛ beyi gɛ elu atɔ̃ piapii pɛ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔɛ alɛ yɛahɔɛ. 5(Onupɛ anɔ teli etinyi kana gɛ ebeɖi yɛahɔɛ bɔpã abhaa pɔ, kɛlɛ yɛabalà eɖi Owusoso Akaa ɔhaa nabha dza?) 6Kɛma ɔsɛ tɛ̀ edzi anɔ gɛ etsyina avɔvɔ, onu gɛnɛ ana, ebefeke yɛishu kɛlɛ ebeku kebhu kukulu mɛ asɛ gɛ abosam tsyɛ ezhieku ana. 7Kɛlɛ sɛ edzi anɔ gɛ bagɛ ama ɔhaa mɛ tsyɛ adɛmɛ yɛibui, sɛ tɛ̀ atɛba mɔ awũi ebui, tɛ̀ ebezhieku abosam kɛgbã mɛ.
8Nɛidzo ɛlɛ nabha, ɔhamɛ ɛzãkɔ bhlui eleteɛ tsyɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ bedzi banyɛ gɛ badɛmɛ alɛ ibui, banɔ gɛ baɖɔ kibui gbã, batɛ lɔkɔ ishu ɔkpɛ bunu gbagbla tɛwɔɛwɔɛ mɛ, pɛ batɛ kpatɔnɔ bɔdɔ̃ pɛpɛ gana edzo kpa abha. 9Kɛlɛ sɛ bedzi banɔ gɛ banyana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã ebui kpukpũɔ gɛedzuɔ mɛ evunɔ nɛitú dadalɛ mɛ. 10Kɛlɛ sɛ beputɔ feke alɛ eɖi, kɛlɛ onupɛ kibui nanà ma balɛishuɔ, tɛ badɛ axɔ̃ɛ asɛ ɛzãkɔ bhlui elete ana.
11Nɛ kidzo ɛlɛ nabha, balɛ edzini alɛ tsyɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ bedzi baadzɛ gɛ banɔ adɛmɛ nɛ alɛ, betinu banɔ gɛ baɖɔ banɔ tibui, pɔ benu badzɛ gɛ belu kitu esi pɛ bayɛkɛ balɛ abha ŋã nɛ kɛka nyia ka mɛ.
12Kɛlɛ sɛ ɔzãkɔ bhlui anɔ eleteɛ edzi anɔ gɛ agbana adzɛ tenikpo, pɛ alà eɖi yɛeyi alɛ nɛ̀ yɛɔpã abha piapii. 13Bagɛ akpɛ ɛtakpu esi piapi nɔ amɔ tɛzazã gagalĩ piapi nɛ̀ kɛklagbu tɔtɔɖɔ nɛ balɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ.
14Gɛ ɛtɔɖɔ ɛlɛ sɛ tɛ ɛba wɔ bha gulũi tsyɛ elo, efɔnɔ ebui alɛ akɔ wɔ sɛ tɛ, 15onupɛ ɛbawɔ kideɛ, obotì kana gɛ kɛlɛ sɛ anɔ azã nɛ̀ ɛkpã nɛ Owusoso Akaa bɔpã mɛ, ɔhaa gɛenu Owusoso Akaa enunuɔ ɔyɛ, kɛgɛ inu bubhi asha, nɛ̀ buvũ esi kpɛkpɛ ɛklagbuɔ ɔkɔ kibui gbã. 16Kɛgɛ dɛ ebui bibisi alɛ petee kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ, Owusoso Akaa mɛ ebuiɛ gɛ abhɛtɛ ibiɛ egbogblo.
Owusoso Akaa ebu yɛishu akasɛ nɛ ishudzya mɛ
Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ bu ɛlɛ ɔkasɛ,
baxɔ̃ɛdɛ amɔ ɛlɛ,
baɖɔ ɛlɛ nɛ amadiɛ nɛishi,
bayɛkɛ ɛlɛ abha ŋã nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ̀,
befeke ɛlɛ avɛnɔ kitití nɛ tɛdadalɛ mɛ.

english_text:
People Will Turn from Their Faith
1God's Spirit clearly says that in the last days many people will turn from their faith. They will be fooled by evil spirits and by teachings that come from demons. 2They will also be fooled by the false claims of liars whose consciences have lost all feeling. These liars 3will forbid people to marry or to eat certain foods. But God created these foods to be eaten with thankful hearts by his followers who know the truth. 4Everything God created is good. And if you give thanks, you may eat anything. 5What God has said and your prayer will make it fit to eat.
Paul's Advice to Timothy
6If you teach these things to other followers, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus. You will show you have grown up on the teachings about our faith and on the good instructions you have obeyed. 7Don't have anything to do with worthless, senseless stories. Work hard to be truly religious. 8-9As the saying goes,
“Exercise is good
for your body,
but religion helps you
in every way.
It promises life
now and forever.”
These words are worthwhile and should not be forgotten. 10We have put our hope in the living God, who is the Savior of everyone, but especially of those who have faith. This is why we work and struggle so hard.
11Teach these things and tell everyone to do what you say. 12Don't let anyone make fun of you, just because you are young. Set an example for other followers by what you say and do, as well as by your love, faith, and purity.
13Until I arrive, be sure to keep on reading the Scriptures in worship, and don't stop preaching and teaching. 14Use the gift you were given when the prophets spoke and the group of church leaders blessed you by placing their hands on you. 15Remember these things and think about them, so everyone can see how well you are doing. 16Be careful about the way you live and about what you teach. Keep on doing this, and you will save not only yourself, but the people who hear you.
translation_text:
Ɔshaa Gɛ Bakɔ Timoteo
1Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ dalɛ ɛlɛ mɛ ɔsɛ nɛ ewui gɔgɔɛ nɔ mɛɛ, beteli aba shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã shu pɛ babà kpatɔnɔ asɛnɛ ɛfɔfuĩa nɛ̀ ebui gɛ ɛfɔfuĩa zizĩɛ kasɛ. 2Bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ ɛlɛ nɛidi de asɛnɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ elete ɔkɔ, banɔ gɛ balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖeɛ atã asɛ gɛ balɔkɔ ɔɖa kpɛkpɛ apɔɛ ɔdɔ̃ ana. 3Bagɛ dɛmɛ asɛ tɛ banɔ agbana adzɛ, kɛlɛ bakpɛ alɛ ɔsha sɛ batɛ ŋã bɔdɔ̃ tuli idi, bɔdɔ̃ gɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ asɛ bagɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã pɛ benyi kibui gbã ayɛkɛ nɛ̀ ahɔɛ huhu. 4Ɔmɔsɛ kɛka nyia ka gɛ Owusoso Akaa abhɛtɛ pɛ̃, kɛlɛ kɛma ɔsɛ bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ kɛnanà shu onupɛ bayɛkɛ kɛka nyia ka nɛ̀ ahɔɛ huhuɔ, 5ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ nɛ̀ yɛ tikukuluɔ kɔ sɛ bɔlɛ ishu dalɛ.
6Onupɛ obu ebui alɛ ɔkasɛ badzyaa, obodzi axɔ̃ɛdɛ gbangbã ɔkɔ Kristo Yesu, gɛ banyɔnɔ nɛ kibui gbã tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ gbangbã gɛ bɔlɛ ede wɔlɛ mɛ. 7Tɛ kpɛ olugbũ nɛ bagɛ ama Owusoso Akaa edeɛ asɛnɛ ebui nanà mɛ, nɛ̀ badzɛ sheshèɛ balɛ ɛdzɛdzɛ tata mɛ, pɔ nyɔnɔ wɔ shu nɛ Owusoso Akaa kidzo nabha. 8Ɔmɔsɛ kedzì yɛyɛkɛ tieli idi akpasɛ ishu dzya bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ mɛ, pɔ Owusoso Akaa mɛ tɛzazã yɛkɛ kedzi ɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ petee, kɛgɛ nyanã kibuiɛ gɛ batɔɖɔ elu ɔkɔ obo kanɔ alɛ, nɛ̀ kanɔ nɔ gɛ kababa mɛ ovunɔ.
9Kɛlɛ nu kibui tɛɖɔɖɔ gɛ yɛkɛ kedzi pɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ bayɛkɛ ɛlɛ 10(ɔmɔsɛ kibui ɛlɛ shu bɔmɔ̃ bɔtɔ pɛ wlɔãkɛ ɔkɔ), sɛ bɔlɔkɔ bɔlɔ otugbà mɛ ɖiɖiɛ ɔkpɛ Owusoso Akaa enunuɔ ahɔɛ mɛ, anɔ gɛ yɛebu banunɔ petee nɛ butsi mɛ, asɛ bɔnɔ nɔ̀ gɛ bayɛkɛ aŋã ana.
11Kɔ ɔsha tɛ̀ ɔkasɛ ebui alɛ. 12Tɛkɔ ɔsɛ ananà eɖi wɔ asɛ anuvɔ wɔonu so, pɔ kpɛ kibui tili olu ɔgana kaɖɔ ɖɔɖɔ mɛ, nɛ wɔɛkpã mɛ, wɔtɛdɛdɛmɛ nɛ̀ anɔ, nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ̀ ɛkpã dadalɛ mɛ. 13Ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ ɛba baa, lɔkɔ wɔ ishu ɔkpɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakla ɔlɛ mɛ nɛ banɔ ɛshi, ɔkɔ kibuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ nɛ̀ kɛlɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ. 14Tɛ shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ wɔ bɔdɔ̃ kɔkɔ gɛ bakɔ wɔ ɔgana ebuiɖɔɖɔ luɔ shu, nɛ kibeɛ gɛ banɔ sheshèɛ ɛzaa nɛ̀ ɔhaa mɛ tɔɖɔ ahɔɛ nɛ wɔabhaa.
15Zã ebui alɛ tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ abha, lɔkɔ wɔ ishuɔ petee kpɛ alɛ tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ, sɛ tɛ anɔ nyia anɔ amɔ wɔ otugbà vɛvɛɛ. 16Lu awɔ̃ nɛ̀ wɔ ɛkpã nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ shu piapii. Kpɛ kemi nɛ bɔlɛ mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ ɔbhɛtɛ ana, obo bu wɔ ishu nɛ bagɛ ɔkasɛ bɔdɔ̃.

english_text:
How To Act toward Others
1Don't correct an older man. Encourage him, as you would your own father. Treat younger men as you would your own brother, 2and treat older women as you would your own mother. Show the same respect to younger women that you would to your sister.
3Take care of any widow who is really in need. 4But if a widow has children or grandchildren, they should learn to serve God by taking care of her, as she once took care of them. This is what God wants them to do. 5 A widow who is really in need is one who doesn't have any relatives. She has faith in God, and she keeps praying to him night and day, asking for his help.
6A widow who thinks only about having a good time is already dead, even though she is still alive.
7Tell all this to everyone, so they will do the right thing. 8People who don't take care of their relatives, and especially their own families, have given up their faith. They are worse than someone who doesn't have faith in the Lord.
9For a widow to be put on the list of widows, she must be at least 60 years old, and she must have been faithful in marriage. 10She must also be well-known for doing all sorts of good things, such as raising children, giving food to strangers, welcoming God's people into her home, helping people in need, and always making herself useful.
11Don't put young widows on the list. They may later have a strong desire to get married. Then they will turn away from Christ 12and become guilty of breaking their promise to him. 13Besides, they will become lazy and get into the habit of going from house to house. Next, they will start gossiping and become busybodies, talking about things that are none of their business.
14I would prefer that young widows get married, have children, and look after their families. Then the enemy won't have any reason to say insulting things about us. 15Look what's already happened to some of the young widows! They have turned away to follow Satan.
16If a woman who is a follower has any widows in her family, she should help them. This will keep the church from having that burden, and then the church can help widows who are really in need.
Church Leaders
17Church leaders who do their job well deserve to be paid twice as much, especially if they work hard at preaching and teaching. 18 It is just as the Scriptures say, “Don't muzzle an ox when you are using it to grind grain.” You also know the saying, “Workers are worth their pay.”
19 Don't listen to any charge against a church leader, unless at least two or three people bring the same charges. 20But if any of the leaders should keep on sinning, they must be corrected in front of the whole group, as a warning to everyone else.
21In the presence of God and Christ Jesus and their chosen angels, I order you to follow my instructions! Be fair with everyone, and don't have any favorites.
22Don't be too quick to accept people into the service of the Lord by placing your hands on them.
Don't sin because others do, but stay close to God.
23Stop drinking only water. Take a little wine to help your stomach trouble and the other illnesses you often have.
24Some people get caught in their sins almost at once, even before the time of judgment. But other people's sins don't show up until later. 25It is the same with good deeds. Some are easily seen, but none of them can be hidden.
translation_text:
Hu Buluhũ Ɔkɔ Bavɛvɛ Edziniɛ Beshesheɛ Nɛ̀ Banɔ Dzidziɛ
1Titseɖenɔ nɛ̀ anɔ sheshè gagalĩ, pɔ hu buluhũ kɔɛ asɛ wɔ akaa ana. Lɔkɔ bunuvɔɛ nɔ asɛ baadzya ana, 2badzɛ sheshèɛ asɛ beelĩ ana, nɛ̀ badzɛnuvɔɛ nɔ asɛ baada ana, tɛ banyana ishu ovunɔ nɛishu dadalɛ mɛ.
3Plɛnɔ bavɛvɛ edzɛniɛ gɛ ɛdɔ̃ nanà ma alɛ ahɔɛ. 4Pɔ onupɛ beyi alo beshui alɛ avɛvɛ yɛedzini ahɔɛɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ banɔ alɛ eputɔ ebu balɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ Akaa mɛ akasɛ, ɔgana kiɖi gɛ beɖi balɛ eshopo alɛ abha pɛ edzinì kɛbhɛ abuia akɔ balɛ elĩ nɛ̀ balɛ akaalɛ, ashuĩ nɛ̀ balɛ edze alɛ, ɔmɔsɛ kɛlɛ ikù Owusoso Akaa nawɔ̃. 5Avɛvɛ yɛedziniɛ gɛ kɛdɔ nana ma yɛahɔɛ pɛ yɛ ete yɛalɛ, yɛotugbà mɛ ɖiɖiɛ petee otsiɖi Owusoso Akaa nabha, kɛlɛ azã ɛlɛ abha elishi nɛ̀ kiwui mɛ tɛ ekulu Owusoso Akaa tɛ ezebisi tepɛplɛnɔ edenɔ yɛahɔɛ mɛ. 6Pɔ avɛvɛ yɛedziniɛ gɛ alɔkɔ ɛkpã ŋaŋã xɔnɔɔ, alɛ nɛkpã pɔ etitsí. 7Lɔkɔ ɔsha lɛ ɔkɔ banɔ nɔ tsyɛ, sɛ tɛ ananà atɛba mɔ̃ bamɛ tsitsí. 8Onupɛ anɔ teli etieɖi yɛeshopo alɛ abhaa, asɛ yɛɔpã mɛ anɛ anaa, agɛ yɛkɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã kɛlɛ ezĩ adɛ anɔ gɛ agɛ yɛkɛŋãa.
9Kɛma ɔsɛ bakalà avɛvɛ yɛedzini nanà akpɛ bavɛvɛ edzini alɛ nɛishi odiyinɔ sɛ eshè afala ɔgɔ afɛtehlo, pɛ abhɛtɛ kibui gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ akɔ yɛenyineɛ 10pɛ benyi yɛ piapii ɔkɔ yɛ ebui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ, asɛ beyi abha tiɖiɖi ana, asɛ beefu tɛyɛyɛkɛ, anɔ gɛ eplu bɔtsɛɖɛkpɔ akɔ banɔ dadalɛ, anɔ gɛ aplɛnɔ bagɛ beku kibui mɛ pɛ alɔkɔ yɛishu akpɛ ebui gbangbã ka nyia ka tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ.
11Bavɛvɛ edzini alɛ gɛenu bubiteɛ xɔnɔɔ, tɛ kalà balɛ ɔkpɛ enyé alɛ nɛishi. Ɔmɔsɛ anyɛ tɛzazãna kibui bakɔsɛ batɛ balà alɔkɔ balɛishu akɔ Kristo, baba pɛ asɛ tɛ bazana anyɛ. 12Kɛlɛ ba mana kebhu kukulu nɛ balɛ ishu abha, ɔmɔsɛ bata esi nɛ balɛ bɔdɔ̃ tatanàa gɛiputɔɔ shu. 13Nɛ kɛlɛ̀ niedeɛ, balɔkɔ balɛishu akpɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ mawɔa mawɔa mɛ, pɛ banyɔ afala ode bɔpã ɔlɛ mɛ ɔvɛnɔ bɔlɛ̀ mɛ. Gɛnɛ sɛ gɛ bedzi kɛxɔ̃ɛ mawɔa mawɔa ete, pɔ baɖɔ banɔ shu tibui pɛ bezĩ banɔ shu, pɛ baɖɔ ebui nɔnɔ gɛ kɛma sɛ tɛ baɖɔɔ. 14Kliso pɛ itseɖe badzɛ gɛ beke shè pɛ benu bavɛvɛ edzini alɛ asɛ bazana anyɛ, tɛ bamɔ eyi, tɛ beɖi balɛ bɔpã abha tɛ batɛkɔ Satana idzo nɛ olugbũ kedzi gagã shu. 15Kibui gbã beteli xɔnɔ eti tsyina pɛ batɔnɔ Satana kukuku.
16Onupɛ adzɛ teli gɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã, pɛ bavɛvɛ yɛedzini alɛ yɛɔpã mɛɛ, akɔ alɛ tɛpɛplɛnɔ tɛ betidzi bɔtɔ ɔkɔ ɔhaa, sɛ tɛ ɔhaa tsyɛ alà aplɛnɔ bavɛvɛ edzini alɛ gɛ bapɛ tɛpɛplɛnɔ gagalĩ nɔ.
17Besheshèɛ gɛ beɖì ɔhaa nabha, kɛshɔnɔ ɔsɛ balɔkɔ kɛtakpu esi kpɛkpɛ gbangbã tili idi akɔ alɛ, asɛ banunɔ gɛ balɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa kɛlɛinu kibuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ nɛ̀ kɛlɛ tɛkakasɛ. 18Ɔmɔsɛ kibuiɛ ɖɔ ɔsɛ, “Nɔtɛnyã olugbũ ɔkɔ enyíɛ gɛ yɛehu amɔɔ, “kɛlɛ” Axɔ̃ɛwɔa ashɔnɔ ɔkɔ yɛbhɛ babuia nɔ.” 19Nɔtɛ obu anɔ sheshè nanà ɛpɔ, odiyinɔ ɔsɛ banɔ taabha alo tata azã amɔ̃ pɛ baɖɔaɖɔ adalɛ ɛlɛ shu. 20Bagɛ abhɛtɛ ebui kpaa xɔnɔɔ, kɛlɛ sɛ betseɖe nɛ balɛ nɛ ɔmaɛshi, sɛ tɛ bagɔgɔ alɔkɔ eɖi balɛ ishu abha nɔ.
21Ɛlɛkɔ ɛlɛ kpɛ wɔahɔɛ sɛ, nɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Kristo Yesu nɛ̀ baxɔ̃ɛdɛ namɛɛ, tɛ ɔnyana ebui alɛ mɛ ovunɔ banɔ amɛ maɖi maɖi, kɛlɛ tɛbhɛtɛ kɛnanà tsyɛ ɔlɔkɔ oku banɔ awɔ̃ nɔ.
22Dɔ fɔ̃ɔ ɔkɔ ahɔɛ tɔtɔɖɔ nɛ anɔ abha, kɛlɛ tɛzã ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ elete ede. Za nɛkpã nɛ wɔshu dadalɛ mɛ.
23Yɔlɔ bulĩ ete tɛwɔwɔ̃ɛ, tɛ ɔwɔ̃ɛ bunu tsyɛ ɛbɔbhlui ɔkɔ wɔ ɛyamɛ gɛtã wɔ nɛ̀ banyã gɛ bakpɛ wɔ buluhũɔ so.
24Banɔ teli ebui kpa gɛ babhɛtɛ abawɔ̃ɛ, gɛ leeku kebhu okulukɔ gɛ ɔlɛ otugbà, ebui kpa gɛ beteli xɔnɔ abhɛtɛ alɛ balɛ ede. 25Nɛidzo ɛlɛ abhaa, ebui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ tsyɛ awɔ̃ɛ, kɛlɛ agɛ atɛawɔ̃ɛ tsyɛɛ, batɛ balà alɛ ekpũ.

english_text:
1If you are a slave, you should respect and honor your owner. This will keep people from saying bad things about God and about our teaching. 2If any of you slaves have owners who are followers, you should show them respect. After all, they are also followers of Christ, and he loves them. So you should serve and help them the best you can.
False Teaching and True Wealth
These are the things you must teach and tell the people to do. 3Anyone who teaches something different disagrees with the correct and godly teaching of our Lord Jesus Christ. 4Those people who disagree are proud of themselves, but they don't really know a thing. Their minds are sick, and they like to argue over words. They cause jealousy, disagreements, unkind words, evil suspicions, 5and nasty quarrels. They have wicked minds and have missed out on the truth.
These people think religion is supposed to make you rich. 6And religion does make your life rich, by making you content with what you have. 7We didn't bring anything into this world, and we won't take anything with us when we leave. 8So we should be satisfied just to have food and clothes. 9People who want to be rich fall into all sorts of temptations and traps. They are caught by foolish and harmful desires that drag them down and destroy them. 10The love of money causes all kinds of trouble. Some people want money so much they have given up their faith and caused themselves a lot of pain.
Fighting a Good Fight for the Faith
11Timothy, you belong to God, so keep away from all these evil things. Try your best to please God and to be like him. Be faithful, loving, dependable, and gentle. 12Fight a good fight for the faith and claim eternal life. God offered it to you when you clearly told about your faith, while so many people listened. 13 Now I ask you to make a promise. Make it in the presence of God, who gives life to all, and in the presence of Christ Jesus, who openly told Pontius Pilate about his faith. 14Promise to obey completely and fully all that you have been told until our Lord Jesus Christ returns.
15 The glorious God
is the only Ruler,
the King of kings
and Lord of lords.
At the time that God
has already decided,
he will send Jesus Christ
back again.
16 Only God lives forever!
And he lives in light
that no one can come near.
No human has ever seen God
or ever can see him.
God will be honored,
and his power
will last forever. Amen.
17Warn the rich people of this world not to be proud or to trust in wealth that is easily lost. Tell them to have faith in God, who is rich and blesses us with everything we need to enjoy life. 18Instruct them to do as many good deeds as they can and to help everyone. Remind the rich to be generous and share what they have. 19This will lay a solid foundation for the future, so they will know what true life is like.
20Timothy, guard what God has placed in your care! Don't pay any attention to godless and stupid talk that sounds smart but really isn't. 21Some people have even lost their faith by believing this talk.
I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
translation_text:
1Banunɔ petee gɛ akpasɛ banɔ dzidzì ɔsha niesiɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ balɛ apalete alɛ, sɛ tɛ̀ batɛ bagã olũgbu kedzi nɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛinyéɛ nɛ̀ yɛɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ shu. 2Bagɛ balɛ apaleteɛ ayɛkɛ Kristo abha ŋã batɛ ɖɔ ɔsɛ badzya benu soɔ batɛ akpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ balɛ apalete alɛ. Nɛ kɛlɛ inye mɛɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ bedzi baxɔ̃ɛdɛ gbangbã ɔkɔ alɛ, ɔmɔsɛ bagɛ amɔ kibui gbangbã odenɔ balɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ enu bayɛyɛkɛŋã, pɛ badɛmɛ alɛ kibui. Ebui alɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ ɔkasɛ tɛ nɔlɔkɔ kpalà alɛ nɔ.
Ehoeyi Tɛdɛdɛmɛ Nɔ̀
3Onupɛ anɔ teli akasɛ asɛnɛ ɔdɔ̃ pɛ agɛ dɛmɛ atɔɖɔ Apalete Yesu Kristo yɛebuiɛ nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa ɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ nabhaa, 4efeke yɛishu kɛlɛ etielũ kɛnanà esi. Enu anɔ gɛ akpɛ same nɛ ebui wɔawlɔ̃akɛ gɛ kesilulũ nanà ama alɛ shuɔ mɛ, ebela pɛpɛɛ nɛ̀ ebui gɛ la mana kiwuhũ dzadzã, anɔ nyanyana kpɛ ɛyamɛ, anɔ shu zizĩ ebui tɛɖɔɖɔ, kizizĩ tɛlɔlɔkɔ nɛ anɔ shu, 5nɛ̀ kawlɔ̃akɛ ebui kibe nyia be nɛ banɔ gɛ balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ agɛ dalɛ nɛishi, banɔ gɛ bagɛ pɛasɛ eti kibui gbã pɛ abhɛtɛ alɛ asɛ Owusoso Akaa mɛ tɛzazã nu kidzo ɔkɔ ehoeyi tɛmɔmɔ.
6Pɔ Owusoso Akaa mɛ tɛzazã gɛixui anɔ nu kɛdɔ̃ gikpe. 7Ɔmɔsɛ bɔgɛ mana kɛdɔ̃ nanà nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ, kɛlɛ bɔtɛ balɔkɔ kɛnanà tsyɛ ɔshɛnɔ nɛ kalɛ mɛ. 8Pɔ onupɛ kɛdɔ̃ ŋaŋã nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kpɛkpɛ lɛ bɔlɔ ahɔɛɛ, nɔkɔ kiku ɔlɔ awɔ̃. 9Banɔ gɛ bapɛ asɛ tɛ bamɔ ehoeyiɛ, bezhieku tifefekeɖi nɛ̀ kɛgbã dɔdɔ mɛ nɛ bɔga dɔdɔ ebui, nɛ̀ ebui kpa gɛ lagbana anɔ kpɛ kizizĩ nɛ̀ butsi mɛ. 10Ɔmɔsɛ ehoeyi tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ alɛenu iguɔ ɔkɔ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ petee. Banɔ teli gɛ bapɛ asɛ tɛ bamɔ ehoeyiɛ, betsyina nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã shu kɛlɛ bakpɛ balɛishu apɔ̃ nɛ̀ ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ gelee.
Kɛgɛ Paulo Akpɛ Ahɔɛ Akɔ Timoteo
11Pɔ wɔ, Owusoso Akaa anɔ, bu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ ɔshɛ nɛ ebui alɛ petee shu, tɛ ɔtɔnɔ ebui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ, Owusoso Akaa mɛ tɛzazã, tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã, tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ, kemikpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ anɔ yɔyɔ tidzidzi ede. 12Bu tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã tɛtsɛɖɛ gbangbã mɛ. Nyana kanɔ tihɔɔ ɛkpã mɛ ovunɔ, agɛ alɛ shu bekulu wɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ ɔdalɛ ishumɛ nɛ banɔ kpekpèɛ gɛ bazã mɔ ebuiɛ namɛ. 13Nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛɛ, anɔ gɛ yɛakɔ kɛdɔ̃ ka nyia ka ɛkpã, nɛ̀ Kristo Yesu, anɔ gɛ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ adalɛ kibuiɛ mɛ nɛ Pontio Pilatoɔ amɛ, adalɛ kibui mɛ piapii, gɛ kɛlɛ mɛlɔkɔ kpɛ wɔahɔɛ 14ɔsɛ tɛ ɔnyana ɔsha mɛ ovunɔ tɛ kihututũ alo kɛpɔ bubu nanà tɛ ba kɔ wɔ ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ Apalete Yesu Kristo ebetsyina aba, 15kɛgɛ Owusoso Akaa aba mana nɛ yɛahɔɛ kibeɛ gɛ atɔɖɔ mɛ, Owusoso Akaa gɛ beyula pɛ yɛete yɛaŋã Ukusiɛ, bekusiɛ petee balɛ Ekusi, nɛ̀ bapaleteɛ petee balɛ Apalete, 16anɔ gɛ yɛete yɛetitsì, nɛ̀ anɔ gɛ akpasɛ kifu gɛ anɔ atɛ balà tsoplɛnɔ nɛishiɛ, anɔ gɛ ananà aka mɔ yɛeɖi alo pɛ abamɔ eɖi tsyɛ. Yɛ kɛlɛ ɔsɛ tɛ bakpɛ yɛinye nɛ̀ yɛohumi nɛitití ɔvɛnɔ tihɔɔ. Amen.
17Kpɛ obo kanɔ alɛ mɛ ehoeyi eleteɛ ɔsha tɛ betifeke ishu alo tɛ balɔkɔ otugbà mɛ tiɖiɖiɛ atɔɖɔ ehoeyi mɔmɔ abha, gɛ betinyi kɛgɛ baba, pɔ balɔkɔ balɛ otugbà mɛ tiɖiɖiɛ atɔɖɔ Owusoso Akaa nabha, anɔ gɛ yɛakɔ bɔlɔ ehoeyi nɛ̀ kɛka nyia ka ɔkɔ bɔlɔ samɛ mɔmɔ. 18Kpɛ alɛ ɔsha sɛ babhɛtɛ kibui gbangbã, sɛ tɛ bamɔ ehoeyi ɔdɔ̃ nɛ ebui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ, nɛ̀ tɛ balɛ ɛyamɛ pɛ̃ tɛ kɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ anɔ zã alɛ mɛ. 19Nɛidzo ɛlɛ abhaa, bebe shogò ehoeyi ɔdɔ̃ akɔ balɛishu asɛ buvũ esi kpɛkpɛ ɛklagbu gɛ gagalĩ ɔkɔ kibèɛ gɛbaa, sɛ tɛ bamɔ ɛkpã gɛ ɛlɛinu ɛkpã gbangbã.
20Timoteo, balɛ gɛdɔɔ gɛ balɔkɔ akpɛ wɔahɔɛ piapii lo. Tsyina ɔshɛ nɛ ebui ɖɔɖɔ nɔnɔ gɛ lagɛ kpaplɛnɔ Owusoso Akaa, nɛ̀ kititì lɔlɔ ebui gɛenu asɛnɛ ebui gɛ baɖɔ asɛ alɛ enu kibui titìɛ shu, 21ebui gɛ banɔ teli baɖɔ elu, kɛlɛ nɛ ebui alɛ tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ mɛɛ, bashɛ nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã niede.
Tibhui bhuibuɔ zã plɛnɔ wɔ o.

english_text:
1From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus.
God himself chose me to be an apostle, and he gave me the promised life that Jesus Christ makes possible.
2 Timothy, you are like a dear child to me. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Christ Jesus will be kind and merciful to you and will bless you with peace!
Do Not Be Ashamed of the Lord
3Night and day I mention you in my prayers. I am always grateful for you, as I pray to the God my ancestors and I have served with a clear conscience. 4I remember how you cried, and I want to see you, because this would make me truly happy. 5 I also remember the genuine faith of your mother Eunice. Your grandmother Lois had the same sort of faith, and I am sure you have it as well. 6So I ask you to make full use of the gift God gave you when I placed my hands on you. Use it well. 7God's Spirit doesn't make cowards out of us. The Spirit gives us power, love, and self-control.
8Don't be ashamed to speak for our Lord. And don't be ashamed of me, just because I am in jail for serving him. Use the power that comes from God and join with me in suffering for telling the good news.
9God saved us and chose us
to be his holy people.
We did nothing
to deserve this,
but God planned it
because he is so kind.
Even before time began
God planned for Christ Jesus
to show kindness to us.
10Now Christ Jesus has come
to offer us God's gift
of undeserved grace.
Christ our Savior defeated death
and brought us
the good news.
It shines like a light
and offers life
that never ends.
11 My work is to be a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher. 12This is why I am suffering now. But I am not ashamed! I know the one I have faith in, and I am sure he can guard until the last day what he has trusted me with. 13Now follow the example of the correct teaching I gave you, and let the faith and love of Christ Jesus be your model. 14You have been trusted with a wonderful treasure. Guard it with the help of the Holy Spirit, who lives within us.
15You know that everyone in Asia has turned against me, especially Phygelus and Hermogenes.
16I pray that the Lord will be kind to the family of Onesiphorus. He often cheered me up and wasn't ashamed of me when I was put in jail. 17Then after he arrived in Rome, he searched everywhere until he found me. 18I pray that the Lord Jesus will ask God to show mercy to Onesiphorus on the day of judgment. You know how much he helped me in Ephesus.
translation_text:
1Paulo, gɛenu Yesu Kristo yɛanɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa tɛpɛpɛ ulũ, nɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ olu ɔkɔ ɛkpã zazãna gɛlɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ,
2Ɛsɛsɛ Timoteo, mɛeyianyɛ gɛ ɛdɛmɛ nɔ keplukpa alɛ,
Tibhui bhuibu, ashagabui ibi nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ odenɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Kristo Yesu gɛenu bɔlɔ Apaleteɛ ɔkɔ.
Kemikpɛkpɛ Banɔ Tɛ̀ Baɖɔ Olugbũ Tonikpo
3Ihu Owusoso Akaa ahɔɛ, anɔ gɛ ɛkpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ, asɛ kana gɛ mɛashuĩ alɛ abhɛtɛ, nɛ̀ ekpeɖekpeɖe dadalɛ mɛ, ɔmɔ asɛ kɛbakà gɛ ɛbakà wɔɛbui elishi nɛ̀ kemuze kibe nyia be nɛ mɛ Owusoso Akaa tikukulu mɛ. 4Ɛbakà ɛwɔmɛ gɛ ozobiɛ, kinu mɛ tɛpɛpɛ sɛ tɛ ɛmɔ wɔ, sɛ tɛ same eku mɛ tsyɛ. 5Batɛkɔ mɛitì nɛ wɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã gbangbã shu, gɛ kiputɔ zã wɔedzé Lois nɛ̀ wɔelĩ Eunike mɛ, kɛlɛ kɛzã mɛuli sɛ kɛkpasɛ wɔ tsyɛ mɛ ana. 6Nɛ kibui ɛlɛ shu soɔ, ɛbaka wɔ sɛ tɛ̀ ɔkpɛ kifù nɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ gɛinu Owusoso Akaa ɛdɔ̃ kɔkɔ gɛkpasɛ wɔ mɛ, ɔgana mɛahɔɛ tɔtɔɖɔ nɛ wɔabha mɛ. 7Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa agɛ kɔ ɔlɔ ɔfɔfuĩa gɛ lodzi yɔɔ nɔ, pɔ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛ ohumi ɔkpasɛ ɔlɛ mɛ pɛ lɔonu tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ, nɛ̀ ishu abha tɛŋaŋã.
8Kliso tɛkɔ kiwũi yi wɔ nɛ Apaleteɛ nɛibuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ shu, alo tɛ kiwũi yi wɔ nɛ mɛ ɔɖavũɔ mɛ gɛ ɛkpasɛ shu. Pɔ plɛnɔ mɛ nɛ bɔtɔ gɛ ɛmɔ nɛ kibui gbangbã shu, nɛ Owusoso Akaa ohumi nɔ shu, 9anɔ gɛ yɛebu ɔlɔ pɛ ekulu bɔlɔ amana ɛkpã gɛ ɛlishu dalɛ mɛ, gɛnɛ kɛnanà gɛ bɔbhɛtɛ shu so, pɔ nɛ yɛ tɛtɔtɔɖɔ nɛ̀ tibhui bhuibuɔ shu so. Bakɔ ɔlɔ tibhui bhuibu ɛlɛ nɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ flogo pɛ kanɔ alɛ mɛ abakpɛ esi, 10pɔ otsi alɛ bebu ɛlɛ akasɛ ɔgana agɛ yayɛkɛ akɔ ɔlɔ tɛbaba mɛ, Kristo Yesu, anɔ gɛ aŋã butsì abha pɛ alɔkɔ ɛkpã gɛ bazã tihɔɔ nɔ amana tɛdadalɛ ɔgana kibui gbangbã mɛ. 11Nɛ kibui gbangbã ɛlɛ shuɔ, bedzu mɛ otugbà anɔ, anɔkpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ adɔkasɛ. 12Kliso pɛ ɛmɔ bɔtɔ ɔlɛ asɛ gɛ ɛmɔ ana. Pɔ kiwũi tiyi mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ inyi anunɔ gɛ yɛabha mɛyɛkɛŋã, kɛlɛ ɛyɛkɛŋã sɛ abalà anyanã kibuiɛ gɛ ɛkpɛ yɛahɔɛ ovunɔ ɔvɛnɔ kiwui ɛlɛ nabha.
13Kɛgɛ nɔolũ odenɔ mɛɔkɔ, nɔnyanà ɛlɛ ovunɔ asɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ piapii, ɔplɛnɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ̀ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ Kristo Yesu mɛ. 14Nyanà kibui gbangbã gɛ balɔkɔ akpɛ wɔ ahɔɛ mɛ ovunɔ piapii, nyanà ɛlɛ mɛ ovunɔ ɔganà Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã gɛ ɔkpasɛ ɔlɔ mɛ tɛpɛplɛnɔ mɛ.
15Onyi ɔsɛ aka nyia aka gɛ akpasɛ Asia ɔdɔ̃ mɛ ashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ mɛishu, ɔlɔkɔ plɛnɔ Figelo nɛ̀ Hermogene tsyɛ.
16Kɛkɔ sɛ tɛ̀ Apaleteɛ ebu yɛashagabui bibiɛ akasɛ Onesiforo yɛɔpã, ɔmɔsɛ akpɛ mɛ kemi kibe nyia be kɛlɛ kiwũi tiyii nɛ ɛɖahuiɛ mɛ gɛ ɛkpasɛ shu. 17Nɛ kɛlɛ shuɔ, gɛ ebeku Roma nɔ, apɛɛ mɛ nɛ̀ awuhũ dzadzã ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ amɔ mɛ. 18Kɛkɔ sɛ tɛ̀ Apaleteɛ aplɛnɔ yɛ tɛ amɔ ashagabui ɖiɖi nɛ kiwui ɛlɛ nabha. Onyi edzo kpekpèɛ gɛ alɛ abha yɛaplɛnɔ mɛ piapii nɛ Efesoɔ.

english_text:
A Good Soldier of Christ Jesus
1Timothy, my child, you must let Christ Jesus make you strong by his gift of undeserved grace. 2You have often heard me teach. Now I want you to tell these same things to followers who can be trusted to tell others.
3As a good soldier of Christ Jesus you must endure your share of suffering. 4Soldiers on duty don't work at outside jobs. They try only to please their commanding officer. 5No one wins an athletic contest without obeying the rules. 6And farmers who work hard are the first to eat what grows in their field. 7If you keep in mind what I have told you, the Lord will help you understand completely.
8Keep your mind on Jesus Christ! He was from the family of David and was raised from death, just as my good news says. 9And because of this message, I am locked up in jail and treated like a criminal. But God's good news isn't locked in jail, 10and so I am willing to put up with anything. Then God's special people will be saved and given eternal glory because they belong to Christ Jesus. 11Here is a true message:
“If we died with Christ,
we will live with him.
12 If we don't give up,
we will rule with him.
If we deny
that we know him,
he will deny
that he knows us.
13If we are not faithful,
he will still be faithful.
Christ cannot deny
who he is.”
An Approved Worker
14Don't let anyone forget these things. And with God as your witness, you must warn them not to argue about words. These arguments don't help anyone. In fact, they ruin everyone who listens to them. 15Do your best to win God's approval as a worker who doesn't need to be ashamed and who teaches only the true message.
16Keep away from worthless and useless talk. It only leads people farther away from God. 17That sort of talk is like a sore that won't heal. And Hymenaeus and Philetus have been talking this way 18by teaching that the dead have already been raised to life. This is far from the truth, and it is destroying the faith of some people.
19But the foundation God has laid is solid. On it is written, “The Lord knows who his people are. So everyone who worships the Lord must turn away from evil.”
20In a large house some dishes are made of gold or silver, while others are made of wood or clay. Some of these are special, and others are not. 21This is also how it is with people. The ones who stop doing evil and make themselves pure will become special. Their lives will be holy and pleasing to their Master, and they will be able to do all kinds of good deeds.
22Run from temptations that capture young people. Always do the right thing. Be faithful, loving, and easy to get along with. Worship with people whose hearts are pure. 23Stay away from stupid and senseless arguments. These only lead to trouble, 24and God's servants must not be troublemakers. They must be kind to everyone, and they must be good teachers and very patient.
25Be humble when you correct people who oppose you. Maybe God will lead them to turn to him and learn the truth. 26They have been trapped by the devil, and he makes them obey him, but God may help them escape.
translation_text:
1Wɔ, mɛeyianyɛ, zã gagalĩi nɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ gɛ kpasɛ Kristo Yesuɔ mɛ. 2Nɛ̀ ebuiɛ gɛ olũ mɛɖɔ nɛ banɔ kpekpèɛ gɛ azã amɔ ebui alɛ amɛ, lɔkɔ ɛlɛ ɔkɔ banɔ gɛ belu awɔ̃ nɛ balɛ abha pɛ balɛ tsyɛ ashɔ̃ asɛ bakasɛ banɔ bhlatɔ ɔdɔ̃. 3Kpɛ emi nɛ ebui gagalĩ mɛ asɛ keyuadɛ akɔ Kristo Yesu ana. 4Anɔ nanà atɛwɔa axɔ̃ɛ asɛ keyuadɛ ana, pɛ aza lɔkɔ yɛishu akpɛ kɛxɔ̃ɛ bhlatɔ wɔawɔa mɛ, onupɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ eku yɛanɔ gbagbla nawɔ̃. 5Kana ɛbeɛ, onupɛ anɔ teli awlɔ̃akɛ nɛ̀ awuhũ dzadzã mɛ asɛ anɔ gɛ ebu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ ana, atɛ yɛkɛ kabha ŋaŋã eleteɛ ikutúɔ ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ apɔ̃ ohuiɛ nɛ kawlɔ̃akɛ ɔsha gɛ bakpɛ nulũ. 6Ayadɛ gɛ awɔa axɔ̃ɛ gagalĩɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ yɛeputɔ ayɛkɛ kɛŋaŋã gɛ bapɔ̃ kawɔ̃pɛ tieli. 7Nɔbaka kibuiɛ gɛ ɛɖɔɔ, ɔmɔsɛ Apaleteɛ abakɔ nolũ ebui alɛ petee esi.
8Baka Yesu Kristoɔ, anɔ gɛ befeke edenɔ bekpuɔ nɛishi pɛ enu David yɛeshui nɔ. Kɛlɛ nu mɛibui gbangbã, 9gɛ kɛlɛ shu mɛmɔ bɔtɔ ɔlɛ vɛnɔ sɛ bakpɛ mɛ nɛ ɛɖahui mɛ asɛ kɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩ elete ana. Pɔ bagɛ kpɛ ɛɖahui nɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ xɔnɔ shu. 10Klisoɔ ɛkpɛ kemi ka nyia ka ɛdi ɔkɔ bagɛ bebuɔ, sɛ tɛ balɛ tsyɛ amɔ tibubuɔ gɛ kpasɛ Yesu Kristoɔ mɛ, nɛ tɛdadalɛ tihɔɔ.
11Kɛlɛ nu kibui gɛlɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ gɛ baɖɔ asɛ,
Onupɛ butsi plɛnɔ yɛɛ,
bɔba zã nɛ ɛkpã nɛ yɛ tsyɛ,
12onupɛ bɔkpɛ kemiɛ,
bɔlɔ tsyɛ baŋã bukusi plɛnɔ yɛ.
onupɛ bɔdã kadã nɛishuɔ,
yɛtsyɛ abadã kadã nɔ ɔlɔ shu,
13onupɛ bɔgɛ yɛkɛ ŋãa,
yɛxɔnɔ abazã tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nabha,
ɔmɔsɛ atɛ balà adã kadã sɛ etinyi yɛishu.
Axɔ̃ɛwɔa Gɛ Owusoso Akaa Atɔɖɔ Ahɔɛ Nɛ Yɛabha
14Zã kɛlɛ abha tɛ ɔzɔ bakà alɛ nɛ̀ ebui alɛ shu. Kpɛ alɛ ɔsha nɛ Owusoso Akaa namɛ nɛ ebelà ebui ɖɔɖɔ shu, gɛ kɛnanà maɛlɛ mɛ, bagɛ elũ ɛlɛ ete kiizĩ. 15Bhɛtɛ piapii tɛ̀ ɔlɔkɔ wɔ shu ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa, asɛ anɔ gɛ batɔɖɔ ahɔɛ nɛ yɛabha ana, axɔ̃ɛwɔa gɛ kɛma sɛ tɛ kiwũi yii pɛ enu axɔ̃ɛwɔa gɛ anyanã kibui gbã mɛ ovunɔ piapi. 16Yɔlɔ kaɖɔɔ gɛetinu Owusoso Akaa shu ebuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ, ɔmɔsɛ banɔ nɔ gɛ balɔkɔ ishu akpɛ kaɖɔ alɛ nɛidi mɛɛ, bebe zĩ atɔɖɔ adɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa shu. 17Balɛ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ bayɛɛ asɛ kɛpɔ̃ ana. Balɛ mɛ beteliɛ balɛenu Himenaio nɛ̀ Fileto, 18banɔ gɛ beyì ide nɛ kibui gbã shu. Baɖɔ asɛ tɛlɔlɔ denɔ bekpuɔ mɛ tɛba bafalanɔ, kɛlɛ bezĩ beteli tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ. 19Pɔ, Owusoso Akaa uvũ esi kpɛkpɛ klagbuɔ xɔnɔ dzi gɛ ɛtɛbà hɔa, pɛ badã ɛlɛ ɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ kibui fɔfɔnɔ ɛlɛ ɔsɛ, “Apaleteɛ enyi bagɛ enu yɛayɛ, kɛlɛ anɔ ka nyia ka gɛ adalɛ Apaleteɛ nɛinye shuɔ, kɛlɛ sɛ eyidè nɛ kɛtakpu mɛ gagalĩ ebui shu.”
20Nɛ bɔpã hohoe mɛɛ, gɛnɛ sikã balɔkɔ abhɛtɛ kɛka nyia ka nɔ, pɔ babhɛtɛ tili tsyɛ nɛ̀ isi nɛ̀ tisi, teli alɛ akɔ ebui dadalɛ kɛlɛ teli tsyɛ alɛ akɔ ebui gɛ lagɛ dalɛ. 21Onupɛ anɔ teli eplu yɛishu edenɔ ebui kukuɔ mɛɛ, ebedzi anɔ gɛ abazã akɔ ebui piapii, gɛ babhɛtɛ ladalɛ, pɛ lapɛ̃ akɔ Apaleteɛ asɛ alɔkɔ awɔa axɔ̃ɛ gbangbã ka nyia ka ɛdi nɔ.
22Bu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ ɔshɛ nɛ bɔyisɔ̃ mɛ ebui zizĩ tɛpɛpɛ shu, tɛ ɔtɔnɔ ebuiɛ gɛ alɛishu adalɛ, gɛenu tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã, tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ ede, ɔlɔkɔ plɛnɔ bagɛ bekulu Apaleteɛ odenɔ kitúɔ gɛ dalɛ mɛ. 23Tɛkpɛ ɔha nɛ̀ bɔga ebui tɛwɔawlɔakɛ, ɔmɔsɛ onyi ɔsɛ lamanà ebelá ebui. 24Pɔ kɛma ɔsɛ Apaleteɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ apɛ ebelá, pɔ, kɛlɛ sɛ yɛyamɛ pɛ ɔkɔ aka nyia aka, kɛlɛ sɛ alà bɔdɔ̃ akasɛ, atɛza akpɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ. 25Bagɛ balɔ kititì nɛishuɔ tsyɛɛ, kɛlɛ sɛ yɛishu mɛ yɔ tɛ ebu ebui esi ɔkɔ alɛ, nɛ̀ kidzo mɛ ɖiɖi ɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa abakɔ alɛ etú mɛ tsyitsyina gɛ ɛba gbana alɛ vɛnɔ kibui titìɛ gɛinu kibui gbã mɛ, 26kɛlɛ babavɛ balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ, pɛ bebu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ ashɛ nɛ abosam kɛgbã shu, anɔ gɛ alɔkɔ alɛ asɛ banɔ dzidzi tɛ̀ babhɛtɛ kɛgɛ yɛapɛɛ nɔ.

english_text:
What People Will Be Like in the Last Days
1You can be certain that in the last days there will be some very hard times. 2People will love only themselves and money. They will be proud, stuck-up, rude, and disobedient to their parents. They will also be ungrateful, godless, 3heartless, and hateful. Their words will be cruel, and they will have no self-control or pity. These people will hate everything good. 4They will be sneaky, reckless, and puffed up with pride. Instead of loving God, they will love pleasure. 5Even though they will make a show of being religious, their religion won't be real. Don't have anything to do with such people.
6Some men fool whole families, just to get power over those women who are slaves of sin and are controlled by all sorts of desires. 7These women always want to learn something new, but they never can discover the truth. 8 Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, these people are enemies of the truth. Their minds are sick, and their faith isn't real. 9But they won't get very far with their foolishness. Soon everyone will know the truth about them, just as Jannes and Jambres were found out.
Paul's Last Instructions to Timothy
10Timothy, you know what I teach and how I live. You know what I want to do and what I believe. You have seen how patient and loving I am, and how in the past I put up with 11 trouble and suffering in the cities of Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. Yet the Lord rescued me from all those terrible troubles. 12In fact, anyone who belongs to Christ Jesus and wants to live right will have trouble from others. 13But evil people who pretend to be what they are not will become worse than ever, as they fool others and are fooled themselves.
14Keep on being faithful to what you were taught and to what you believed. After all, you know who taught you these things. 15Since childhood, you have known the Holy Scriptures that are able to make you wise enough to have faith in Christ Jesus and be saved. 16Everything in the Scriptures is God's Word. All of it is useful for teaching and helping people and for correcting them and showing them how to live. 17The Scriptures train God's servants to do all kinds of good deeds.
translation_text:
Owusoso Akaa Matimatì Nɛ Ewui Gɔgɔɛ Nɔ Mɛ̀
1Pɔ kpɛ kibui ɛlɛ awɔ̃ olu ɔsɛ. Kibè kpa babà nɛ ewui gɔgɔɛ nɔ mɛ. 2Baanɔ abadɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ balɛishu, banɔ aba adɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ ehoeyi, bebetũ ahɔɛ nɛitélũ, bebe feke ishu, babà kwɔalà ɔdɔ̃, babà gagalĩ atɔ̃ mɛ nɛ balɛ elĩ nɛ̀ balɛ akaa alɛ shu, ahɔɛ mahumahu, ishu madalɛ madalɛ, 3tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ mazã mazã, batɛ balɔkɔ kibui etsye anɔ, bebezĩ banɔ shu, batɛ balà balɛishu abha aŋã, atakpu mɛ gagalĩ ebui, batɛ badɛmɛ nɔ̀ nɛ̀ kibuiɛ gɛpɛ̃, 4babà kpɛ anɔ ahɔɛ, babà pɛ bɔdɔ̃ ziazia, baba yɛkɛ balɛishu abha aŋã, baba dɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ kanɔ mɛ same mɔmɔ ebui ɔdɛ Owusoso Akaa- 5babà abhɛtɛ ishu asɛ Owusoso Akaa ibuiɛ kpasɛ alɛ mɛ, pɔ babà shɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ kɛlɛ ohumi nɔ shu. Tɛ kpɛ alɛ ɔha.
6Benu banɔ ɛdi tili gɛ shɔku bapãmɛ asɛ kɛsɔ̃ pɛ baŋã badzɛ gɛ ohumi ɔma balɛ ahɔɛ nabhaa, badzɛ gɛ apɛ ana, pɛ ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ akpasɛ alɛ mɛ pɛ bawɔɛ alɛ nɛ̀ ebui kpa ka nyia ka ɛdi tɛpɛpɛ mɛ, 7babà kpasɛ bɔdɔ̃ kibe nyia be pɔ batɛ bamɔ kibui gbã alɔkɔ elu eɖi. 8Asɛ kana gɛ Yane nɛ̀ Yambre alɔɛtití nɛ Moses shuɔ, kana ɛbe banɔ alɛ tsyɛ abalɔɛtití nɛ kibui gbã shu-banɔ gɛ balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe mɛ agɛ dalɛ, banɔ gɛ bashɛnɛ ahɔɛ nɛ balɛishu nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ. 9Pɔ batɛ bamɔ otugbà vɛvɛ, ɔmɔsɛ asɛ kana gɛ kɛlɛ nɛ banɔ alɛ shuɔ, balɛ bɔga dɔdɔ mɛ badalɛ ɔkɔ aka nyia aka.
Kɛxɔ̃ɛ Gɔgɔɛ Nɔ Gɛ Paulo Akɔ Timoteo
10Wɔ, onyi ibui odenɔ mɛɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ petee shu, mɛ ɛkpã gɛ idzinɔ, kɛgɛ ɛtɔɖɔ, mɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã, mɛ kitú esi lulu, mɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ, mɛ kemì tɛkpɛkpɛ, 11kede tɛtɔtɔnɔ, mɛ bɔtɔ mɔmɔ, ebui nɔnɔ ɛdi gɛ laba mɛabha nɛ Antioxia, Ikonio nɛ̀ Listra nɔ, kede tɔtɔnɔ nɔnɔ ɛdi gɛ ɛkpɛ kemi nɛ alɛ mɛ. Pɔ Apaleteɛ ebu mɛ edenɔ alɛ petee mɛ. 12Nɛ kibui gbã mɛɛ, babatɔnɔ anɔ ka nyia ka gɛ apɛ asɛ tɛ azãna ɛkpã kɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Yesu Kristoɔ niede, 13kɛlɛ banɔ kpa nɛ̀ bagɛ abhɛtɛ ishu asɛ bapɛ xɔnɔ ebèzĩ atɔɖɔ, babawɔɛ banɔ kɛlɛ babawɔɛ alɛ tsyɛ. 14Pɔ wɔ xɔnɔɔ, zã ɛlɛ abha ɔzɔbhɛtɛ kɛgɛ ɔkpasɛ, pɛ olũ kɛlɛ esiɛ, ɔmɔsɛ onyi banɔ alɛ gɛ balɛ ɔkɔ wɔ kpasɛ alɛ odenɔ, 15kɛlɛ nɛ kana gɛ onyi Owusoso Akaa kibui odenɔ kinuvɔbe soɔ, kibui gɛ bhɛtɛ wɔ pɛ odzì ebuitinɛ ɔkɔ wɔ tibubu ɔgana tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ Kristo Yesu abha. 16Ebui fɔfɔnɔ luɔ petee enu Owusoso Akaa ɛbui gɛ afuĩa kpɛ ɛlɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ kɛpɛ̃ ɔsɛ balɔkɔ ɛlɛ akasɛ bɔdɔ̃ nɔ, tɛ̀ balɔkɔ ɛlɛ etseɖe nɔ nɛ̀ anɔ, tɛ balɔkɔ akpalà anunɔ, tɛ bewoese anɔ nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ nɛ ishu dadalɛ mɛ, 17sɛ tɛ Owusoso Akaa anunɔ abalɔkɔ ɛlɛ epo yɛishu nɔ ɔkɔ kɛxɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa gbangbã ka nyia ka ɛdi.

english_text:
1When Christ Jesus comes as king, he will be the judge of everyone, whether they are living or dead. So with God and Christ as witnesses, I command you 2to preach God's message. Do it willingly, even if it isn't the popular thing to do. You must correct people and point out their sins. But also cheer them up, and when you instruct them, always be patient. 3The time is coming when people won't listen to good teaching. Instead, they will look for teachers who will please them by telling them only what they are itching to hear. 4They will turn from the truth and eagerly listen to senseless stories. 5But you must stay calm and be willing to suffer. You must work hard, telling the good news and to do your job well.
6Now the time has come for me to die. My life is like a drink offering being poured out on the altar. 7I have fought well. I have finished the race, and I have been faithful. 8So a crown will be given to me for pleasing the Lord. He judges fairly, and on the day of judgment he will give a crown to me and to everyone else who wants him to appear with power.
Personal Instructions
9Come to see me as soon as you can. 10 Demas loves the things of this world so much that he left me and went to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia. 11 Only Luke has stayed with me.
Mark can be very helpful to me, so please find him and bring him with you. 12 I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
13 When you come, bring the coat I left at Troas with Carpus. Don't forget to bring the scrolls, especially the ones made of leather.
14 Alexander, the metalworker, has hurt me in many ways. But the Lord will pay him back for what he has done. 15Alexander opposes what we preach, so you had better watch out for him.
16When I was first put on trial, no one helped me. In fact, everyone deserted me. I hope it won't be held against them. 17But the Lord stood beside me. He gave me the strength to tell his full message, so that all Gentiles would hear it. And I was kept safe from hungry lions. 18 The Lord will always keep me from being harmed by evil, and he will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. Praise him forever and ever! Amen.
Final Greetings
19 Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila and to the family of Onesiphorus.
20 Erastus stayed at Corinth.
Trophimus was sick when I left him at Miletus.
21Do your best to come before winter.
Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, and Claudia send you their greetings, and so do the rest of the Lord's followers.
22I pray that the Lord will bless your life and will be kind to you.
translation_text:
1Nɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Kristo Yesu amɛɛ, anunɔ gɛ yɛebè kulu kebhu nɛ̀ bagɛ alɛ nɛkpã nɛ betsitsiɛ, kɛlɛ nɛ yɛ tɛbaba nɛ yɛikusi ɔŋãkɔɔ, ɛkɔ wɔ ɔsha ɔlɛ sɛ, 2Ɖɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ, balɛ sɛ onupɛ banɔ apɛ asɛ tɛ belũ kibuiɛ alo tɛ betielũ ɛlɛ tsyɛo, woese anɔ, tseɖe nɛ̀ anɔ tɛ̀ ɔkpɛ banɔ emi-nɛ kitu esi lulũ nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ piapii mɛ. 3Ɔmɔsɛ kibèɛ baba gɛ, banɔ azapɛ asɛ tɛ belũ batɔ̃ akɔ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ piapii nɔ. Pɔ tɛ beku ishu awɔ̃ soɔ, babalɔkɔ badɔ̃ kakasɛ kpekpè akana balɛ ishu tɛ betseɖe alɛ kɛgɛ batɔ̃ gɛ awɔɛsɛ̃ alɛ apɛɛ asɛ tɛ belũɔ. 4Bebè tsyina balɛ atɔ̃mɛ ashɛnɔ nɛ kibui gbã shu kɛlɛ bebè tsyina avɛ ɛdzɛdzɛ ebui shu. 5Pɔ wɔ xɔnɔɔ, zã gagalĩi nɛ kɛka nyia ka mɛ, kpɛ emi nɛ ebui gagalĩ mɛ, wɔa kɛxɔ̃ɛ asɛ anɔ gɛ akanà kibuiɛ ana, wɔa axɔ̃ɛ petee ɔkɔ wɔ ɔhaa.
6Ɔmɔsɛ batɛ lɔkɔ mɛ xɔnɔ a kpɛ ahɔɛ asɛ beyí, kɛlɛ kibeɛ na kɔmɛ tɛshɛshɛ. 7Ɛta keyu gbangbã, ibu tɛtsɛɖɛ mɛ beke, ɛnyana mɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ vunɔ. 8Otsi alɛ xɔnɔ, balɔkɔ kibui gbangbã tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ kikutuɔ abalɛ elu akɔ mɛ, kɛgɛ Apalete gɛenu kebhu gbangbã kukulu eleteɛ, abalɔkɔ akɔ mɛ nɛ kiwui ɛlɛ nabhaa, kɛlɛ gɛnɛ mɛ ete yɛabakɔ, pɔ abakɔ bagɛ eɖi yɛ tɛbaba idzo mɛ tsyɛ.
Kɛgɛ Ɛmɔlu Nɛ Mɛishu Ahɔɛ Mɛ
9Bhɛtɛ kɛgɛ pɛɔdɛ tɛ ɔba mɛɔkɔ tsĩɔɔ, 10Dema xɔnɔ gɛ adɛmɛnɔ nɛ̀ kanɔ alɛ mɛ soɔ, ashɛ ayɔlɔ mɛ avɛ Tesalonika. Kreske tsyɛ avɛ Galatia, kɛlɛ Tito tsyɛ avɛ Dalmatia. 11Luka ete yɛalɛ mɛɔbha. Nɔpɛ Marko tɛ nɔgbanà yɛɔmana ɔmɔsɛ akɔ mɛ tɛpɛplɛnɔ nɛ mɛ axɔ̃ɛ wɔawɔa mɛ. 12Ɛsɛsɛ Tixiko nɛ Efeso. 13Onupɛ ɔbaa, lɔkɔ mɛ akpɛkpɛ dzloloɔ gɛ ɛshɛ yɔlɔ nɛ Karpo ɔkɔ nɛ̀ Troa nɔɔ, mana mɛ, ɔlɔkɔ plɛnɔ buplukpa nɛ̀ bagã bupiɛ.
14Aleksandro gɛ anɔ bɔdɔ̃ɔ, abhɛtɛ kibui kpa tili nɛ mɛ. Apaleteɛ aba bhuia kɛbhɛ ɔkɔ yɛ nɛ kɛgɛ yɛabhɛtɛ shu. 15Wɔ tsyɛ ɖi wɔ ishu abha piapi nɛ yɛishu, ɔmɔsɛ alɔɛtití nɛ bɔlɔ ebuiɛ shu gagalĩi.
16Nɛ mɛ ishu ulũ bubuɔ gɛ iputɔ shuɔ, ananà agɛba plɛnɔ mɛ, pɔ aka nyia aka ashɛ ayɔlɔ mɛ. Batɛ̀ lɔkɔ ɛlɛ abuia kɛbhɛ akɔ alɛ nɔ. 17Pɔ, Apaleteɛ azã aplɛnɔ mɛ pɛ akpɛ mɛ ohumi, sɛ tɛ ɔgana mɛabhaa, baba ɖɔ kibuiɛ akana, kɛlɛ bagɛ betinu Yudanɛ tsyɛ ebelũ ɛlɛ. Kɛlɛ bebu mɛ edenɔ dzata olugbũ mɛ. 18Apaleteɛ ebe bu mɛ odenɔ kibui kpa ka nyia ka ɛdi gɛ balɔkɔ nɛ mɛ shuɔ mɛ, kɛlɛ aba gbanà mɛ avɛnɔ yɛititì bukusi ɔŋãkɔ nɛ ishu yɔyɔ mɛ. Ishu dadalɛ ɔkɔ yɛ ɔvɛnɔ tihɔɔ. Amen.
Bɔdɔ̃ Sɛsɛnɔ Gɔgɔɛ Nɔ
19Sɛnɔ Priskila nɛ̀ Akwila nɛ̀ Onesiforo balɛ ɔpã mɛ anɛ ɔkɔ mɛ. 20Erasto azã Korinto, kɛlɛ ɛshɛ yɔlɔ Trofimo gɛ adɔ bɔnyã nɛ Mileto. 21Ɖi ɛlɛ ɔkɔ sɛ obo ku ɛkɛlɛ flogo pɛ ekukùɔ ana. Eubulo, Pude, Lino nɛ̀ Klaudia nɛ badzya petee asɛnɛ̀ wɔ.
22Apaleteɛ azã plɛnɔ wɔ ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ. Tibhui bhuibuɔ zã plɛnɔ wɔ o.

english_text:
1From Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ.
I encourage God's own people to have more faith and to understand the truth about religion. 2Then they will have the hope of eternal life God promised long ago. And God never tells a lie! 3So, at the proper time, God our Savior gave this message and told me to announce what he had said.
4 Titus, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and Christ Jesus our Savior will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
What Titus Was To Do in Crete
5I left you in Crete to do what had been left undone and to appoint leaders for the churches in each town. As I told you, 6 they must have a good reputation and be faithful in marriage. Their children must be followers of the Lord and not have a reputation for being wild and disobedient.
7Church officials are in charge of God's work, and so they must also have a good reputation. They must not be bossy, quick-tempered, heavy drinkers, bullies, or dishonest in business. 8Instead, they must be friendly to strangers and enjoy doing good things. They must also be sensible, fair, pure, and self-controlled. 9They must stick to the true message they were taught, so their good teaching can help others and correct everyone who opposes it.
10There are many who don't respect authority, and they fool others by talking nonsense. This is especially true of some Jewish followers. 11But you must make them be quiet. They are after money, and they upset whole families by teaching what they should not. 12It is like one of their own prophets once said,
“The people of Crete
always tell lies.
They are greedy and lazy
like wild animals.”
13This surely is a true saying. And you should be hard on such people, so you can help them grow stronger in their faith. 14Don't pay any attention to any of those senseless Jewish stories and human commands. These are made up by people who won't obey the truth.
15Everything is pure for someone whose heart is pure. But nothing is pure for an unbeliever with a dirty mind. That person's mind and conscience are destroyed. 16Such people claim to know God, but their actions prove they really don't. They are disgusting. They won't obey God, and they are too worthless to do anything good.
translation_text:
1Paulo, anɔ gɛedzì axɔ̃ɛdɛ akɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ anɔkpɛkpɛ akɔ Yesu Kristo ɔkɔ bagɛ Owusoso Akaa ebu alɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã, nɛ̀ kibuititìɛ gɛlɛ kibui gbã shu pɛ kɛgbanà anɔ vɛnɔ Akaa mɛ tɛzazã, 2tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã nɛ̀ kibuititiɛ gɛipo ɛkpã tihɔɔ gɛ buɖi kidzo mɛ, gɛ Owusoso Akaa, anɔ gɛ atɛ sɛ asɛnɛ, atɔɖɔ elu akɔ ɔlɔ flogo pɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ abakpɛ esi, 3kɛlɛ nɛ yɛ kibeɛ gɛ atɔɖɔ eluɔ shuɔ, amana yɛibuiɛ nɛ tɛdadalɛ mɛ ɔgana kibuiɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ gɛ balɔkɔ akpɛ mahɔɛ ɔgana Owusoso Akaa gɛ ebu ɔlɔ yɛɔsha kpɛkpɛ mɛ,
4Ɛlɔkɔ asɛsɛ wɔ Tito, mɛeyianyɛ gɛ ɔkpasɛ bɔlɔ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ,
Tibhui bhuibu nɛ̀ ishu yɔyɔ de Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Kristo Yesu gɛenu agɛ yɛebu ɔlɔ kɔ.
Kɛxɔ̃ɛ Gɛ Bakɔ Tito Nɛ Kreta
5Kɛnɔnɔ shu so pɛ ɛshɛ yɔlɔ wɔ nɛ Kreta kɛlɛinu ɔsɛ tɛ owoese ebuiɛ gɛatɔ̀ pɛ balɔkɔ akpɛ wɔahɔɛ tɛ obu bagɛ abazã ɔha nulũ nɛ ɔma ka nyia ka mɛ, asɛ kana gɛ ɛkasɛ wɔ ana. 6Kɛma sɛ tɛ kɛpɔ bubu zã osofo shu, edzi anɔ gɛ agbana adzɛ tenikpo, anyɛ gɛ yɛeyi alɛ ayɛkɛ aŋã pɛ betinu banɔ gɛ babhɛtɛ agbagbà ebui pɛ betielũ atɔ̃. 7Gɛ balɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa ɛxɔ̃ɛ akpɛ osofo ahɔɛ soɔ, kɛma sɛ tɛ bamɛ tsitsí ebui azã yɛishu-kɛma ɔsɛ tɛ eze feke yɛishu, etidzi anɔ gɛ akpɛ ɔmɔpɔɛ nɛ ebui mɛ, etidzi enuwɔ̃ɛ, etidzi ebelapɛ, etidzi anɔ gɛ apɛ kanɔ mɛ ɔdɔ̃ ɔgana kidzo kpa abha. 8Pɔ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ yɛyamɛ pɛ̃, anɔ gɛ adɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ kɛgɛ pɛ, anɔ gɛ aŋã yɛishu abha, aɖɔ kibui nɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ, yɛishu dalɛ pɛ anyana yɛishu evunɔ. 9Kɛlɛ sɛ anyana kibuiɛ gɛ lɛ ɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ pɛ balɔkɔ akpɛ yɛahɔɛ mɛ ovunɔ gagalĩ asɛ gɛ bakasɛ yɛana, sɛ tɛ alà akpɛ bagɔgɔɛ nɔ emi nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ piapi tɛ etilũ bagɛ balɔ kititì nɛishuɔ atɔ̃.
10Ɔmɔsɛ batɔ̃mɛ gagalĩ elete kpekpè alɛ, banɔ gɛ baɖɔaɖɔ ete, pɛ bawɔɛ anɔ, banɔ asɛ bagɛ bakpasɛ Yudanɛ ɛkɔɔ mɛ ana. 11Kɛlɛ sɛ bakɔ bata atɔ̃, ɔmɔsɛ bezĩ bapãmɛ nɛ̀ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ gɛma ɔsɛ bakasɛ ɔkɔ kidzo kpa abha ɔdɔ̃ mɔmɔ. 12Balɛ ahɔɛ ebuiɖɔluanɔ tenikpo gɛ ede Krete nɔ tsyɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Kretanɛ enu basɛnɛ elete ɖaa, ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ elete nɛ̀ banɔ zhyɔzhyɔrɛ elete gɛ baŋã bɔdɔ̃ ɔtsɔ̃.” 13Kibui ɛlɛ shu gɛ baɖɔaɖɔ nu kibui gbã. Klisoɔ, notseɖe nɛ alɛ odunu ɔlɛ, sɛ tɛ bazã piapi nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã shu 14tɛ batɛ taatɔ̃ nɛ̀ Yudanɛ asɛnɛ ebuiɛ, alo bagɛ atɛapɛ kibui gbã asɛ elũɔ ɔsha. 15Nɛ banɔ gɛ balɛishu dalɛ shuɔ, kɛka nyia ka shu dalɛ, pɔ nɛ bagɛ batsɔmɔ̃ pɛ bagɛ yɛkɛ aŋã ɔkɔ xɔnɔ, kɛnanà shu gɛ dalɛ. Nɛ kibui gbã mɛ, balɛ ekpeɖekpeɖe nɛ̀ balɛ etúɔ mɛ atsɔmɔ. 16Baɖɔ asɛ enyi Owusoso Akaa, pɔ nɛ balɛ ebui bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ xɔnɔɔ, betinyi yɛ. Banyana anɔ akpɛ ɛyamɛ, betielũ atɔ̃, kɛlɛ bagɛ na ɔkɔ kɛgbangbã nanà bhɛbhɛtɛ.

english_text:
Instructions for Different Groups of People
1Titus, you must teach only what is correct. 2Tell the older men to have self-control and to be serious and sensible. Their faith, love, and patience must never fail.
3Tell the older women to behave as those who love the Lord should. They must not gossip about others or be slaves of wine. They must teach what is proper, 4so the younger women will be loving wives and mothers. 5Each of the younger women must be sensible and kind, as well as a good homemaker, who puts her own husband first. Then no one can say insulting things about God's message.
6Tell the young men to have self-control in everything.
7Always set a good example for others. Be sincere and serious when you teach. 8Use clean language that no one can criticize. Do this, and your enemies will be too ashamed to say anything against you.
9Tell slaves always to please their owners by obeying them in everything. Slaves must not talk back to their owners 10or steal from them. They must be completely honest and trustworthy. Then everyone will show great respect for what is taught about God our Savior.
God's Kindness and the New Life
11God has shown us undeserved grace by coming to save all people. 12He taught us to give up our wicked ways and our worldly desires and to live decent and honest lives in this world. 13We are filled with hope, as we wait for the glorious return of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ. 14 He gave himself to rescue us from everything evil and to make our hearts pure. He wanted us to be his own people and to be eager to do right.
15Teach these things, as you use your full authority to encourage and correct people. Make sure you earn everyone's respect.
translation_text:
Kɛgɛ Ɛlɛ Sɛ Bakasɛ Aza Ediediɛ
1Kɛlɛ sɛ ɔkasɛ kɛgɛ shɔnɔ pɛ kinu bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ gbangbã. 2Kasɛ banɔ sheshèɛ tɛ belu kitu esi, sɛ tɛ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ alɛ, ishu abha ŋaŋã, bazã piapi nɛ tɛyɛyɛkɛŋã mɛ, nɛ tɛdɛdɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ kemikpɛkpɛ mɛ.
3Kana ɛbe, kasɛ badzɛ sheshèɛ tɛ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi nɛ kana gɛ baba zana ɛkpã shu, betidzi banɔ gɛ agã olugbũ edzi alo banɔ gɛ bawɔ̃ɛ bunu ebuku, pɔ tɛ bakasɛ kɛgɛ pɛ̃. 4Kɛlɛɛ babalà kasɛ badzɛnuvɔɛ nɔ tsyɛ ɔsɛ tɛ badɛmɛ nɔ nɛ̀ balɛ enyine nɛ̀ balɛ eyi alɛ, 5tɛ baŋã balɛishu abha tɛ balɛishu dalɛ, tɛ bazadɛ bɔpã mɛ axɔ̃ɛ, tɛ balɛ ɛyamɛ pɛ, tɛ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ balɛ enyine alɛ, sɛ tɛ ananà atɛɖɔ kaɖɔ kpà nɛ Owusoso Akaa nɛibuiɛ shu.
6Kidzo ɛlɛ nabha, nɔkpɛ bayisɔ̃ nɔ emi tɛ baŋã balɛishu abha. 7Nɛ kɛka nyia ka mɛɛ, bhɛtɛ ibui ɔkasɛ alɛ ɔganà wɔɛbui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ mɛ. Nɛ wɔ bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ mɛɛ, kasɛ kana gɛ bazã nɛkpã, nɛ̀ bamɛ gagalĩ. 8Kasɛ kaɖɔ gbangbã gɛ batɛ balà lɔɛtití nɛ alɛ shu tɛɖɔɖɔ, sɛ tɛ kiwũi yi bagɛ balɔɛtití nɛ wɔ shuɔ, ɔmɔsɛ bagɛ mɔ kibui kpa nanà gɛ abaɖɔ nɛ bɔlɔ shu.
9Kasɛ banɔ dzidziɛ ɔdɔ̃ tɛ bakpɛ kɛtakpu esi akɔ balɛ apalete alɛ nɛ kɛka nyia ka mɛ, babhɛtɛ tɛ beku alɛ awɔ̃, tɛɖɔ kaɖɔ kpa kpɛ balɛ ɔkɔ, 10kɛlɛ beti zuru alɛ, pɔ kasɛ alɛ ɔsɛ ɔyɛkɛ alɛ abha ɔŋã, sɛ tɛ nɛ kidzo ka nyia ka abha bɔdɔ̃ kakasɛ gɛ bayɛkɛ nɛ Owusoso Akaa agɛ yɛebu ɔlɔ shu tɛ̀ apɔ ɔɖi.
11Ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa tibhui bhuibuɔ gɛ mana tibubuɔ koeyì ɔkɔ banɔ nɔ petee. 12Kɛkasɛ ɔlɔ sɛ bɔɖɔ sɛ, “Ao” nɛ kɛgɛ gɛ kpaplɛnɔ Owusoso Akaa pɛ kinu kanɔ mɛ ebui tɛpɛpɛ shu, nɛ tɛ̀ bɔŋã bɔlɔ shu abha, bɔɖɔ olugbũ tonikpo tɛ bɔzana ɛkpã ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ obo kanɔ alɛ mɛ, 13nɛ kibeɛ gɛ buɖi kidzo mɛ ɔkɔ tiyuyula gɛ buɖi kidzo mɛ ɔkɔɔ, nɛ kibeɛ gɛ bupo tiyuyula ɛwuiɛ gɛ buɖi kidzo mɛ ɔkɔ nɛ Owusoso Akaa gbagbla yɛ tɛdadalɛ nɛ̀ Yesu Kristo gɛ ebu ɔlɔ tɛbaba nabha, 14anɔ gɛ yɛalɔkɔ yɛishu akɔ asɛ tɛ̀ ebu ɔlɔ denɔ atakpu mɛ gagalĩɛ petee mɛ. Tɛ̀ epulu ɔlɔ akɔ yɛishu asɛ banɔ gɛenu yayɛ, banɔ gɛ apɛɛ asɛ tɛ̀ babhɛtɛ kɛgɛ pɛ.
15Ebui alɛ kɛlɛ sɛ tɛ ɔkasɛ. Kpɛ balɛ emi tɛ̀ ɔlɔkɔ ɔshakpɛkpɛ petee otseɖenɔ nɛ alɛ. Tɛkɔ ananà amɔ wɔ esi.

english_text:
Doing Helpful Things
1Remind your people to obey the rulers and authorities and not to be rebellious. They must always be ready to do something helpful 2and not say cruel things or argue. They should be gentle and kind to everyone. 3We used to be stupid, disobedient, and foolish, as well as slaves of all sorts of desires and pleasures. We were evil and jealous. Everyone hated us, and we hated everyone.
4God our Savior showed us
how good and kind he is.
5He saved us because
of his mercy,
and not because
of any good things
we have done.
God washed us by the power
of the Holy Spirit.
He gave us new birth
and a fresh beginning.
6God sent Jesus Christ
our Savior
to give us his Spirit.
7Jesus treated us much better
than we deserve.
He made us acceptable to God
and gave us the hope
of eternal life.
8This message is certainly true.
These teachings are useful and helpful for everyone. I want you to insist that the people follow them, so that all who have faith in God will be sure to do good deeds. 9But don't have anything to do with stupid arguments about ancestors. And stay away from disagreements and quarrels about the Law of Moses. Such arguments are useless and senseless.
10Warn troublemakers once or twice. Then don't have anything else to do with them. 11You know their minds are twisted, and their own sins show how guilty they are.
Personal Instructions and Greetings
12 I plan to send Artemas or Tychicus to you. After he arrives, please try your best to meet me at Nicopolis. I have decided to spend the winter there.
13 When Zenas the lawyer and Apollos get ready to leave, help them as much as you can, so they won't have need of anything.
14Our people should learn to spend their time doing something useful and worthwhile.
15Greetings to you from everyone here. Greet all of our friends who share in our faith.
I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
translation_text:
Kɛgbangbã Tɛbhɛbhɛtɛ
1Baka banunɔ ɔsɛ bezhinì ishu esi akɔ bekusiɛ nɛ̀ ɔshaeleteɛ, tɛ belũ atɔ̃, tɛ babalɛ elù asɛ babà abhɛtɛ kibui gbangbã, 2tɛ̀ batɛ gã olugbũ kedzi nɛ ananà shu, bazã ishu yɔyɔ mɛ tɛ beɖi anɔ ashagabui, tɛ bezhini ishu esi ɔkɔ aka nyia aka.
3Kibè tili gɛ bafala, bɔlɔ tsyɛ zɛdɔ bɔga, bugizi lũ atɔ̃, bawɔɛ ɔlɔ budzi banɔ dzidzi ɔkɔ kanɔ mɛ bɔdɔ̃ nɛ̀ kalɛ mɛ same ebuiɛ petee. Bɔzana ɛkpã kpa kɛlɛ bɔkpɛ banɔ awɔ̃, bɔkɔ sɛ banɔ anyanã ɔlɔ akpɛ ɛyamɛ kɛlɛ bɔlɔ tsyɛ nyanã bagɔlɔ kpɛ ɛyamɛ. 4Pɔ nɛ kibeɛ gɛ ɛyamɛ gbangbã gɛide Owusoso Akaa gɛ yɛebu ɔlɔ ekoeyiɛ, 5ebu ɔlɔ, gɛnɛ nɛ bɔlɔ ebui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ shu so, pɔ nɛ yɛ ashagabui ɖiɖiɛ shu so. Ebu ɔlɔ ɔgana tiwuwlunɔ odenɔ tɛmɔmɔ vɔvɔ, nɛ̀ Ɔfɔfuĩa Gbangbã gɛ lodzinì ɔlɔ mɔ́ avɔvɔ mɛ, 6ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ gɛ alɔkɔ ɛyamɛ gbangbã abalɛ̀ bɔlɔ abha ɔgana Yesu Kristoɔ gɛ ebu ɔlɔ nabha, 7sɛ tɛ, onupɛ bebhui bɔtsyɔa akɔ ɔlɔ gana yɛ tibhui bhuibuɔ mɛɛ, bɔbadzi kaŋãsi elete gɛ bɔbà ziɖi otugbàa mɛ ɔkɔ ɛkpã tihɔɔ nɔ. 8Kibui ɛlɛ tɛɖɔɖɔ nu kibui gbã. Kɛlɛ ɛpɛ sɛ tɛ ɔɖɔ ebui alɛ odzinɔ ɔɖɔ, sɛ tɛ bagɛ ayɛkɛ Owusoso Akaa nabha aŋãa beɖi ishu abha piapii tɛ̀ balɔkɔ balɛishu akɔ kibui gbangbã bhɛbhɛtɛ. Ebui alɛ apɛ̃ kɛlɛ labà manà kibui gbangbã ɔkɔ aka nyia aka.
9Pɔ yɔlɔ bɔga ebui wɔawlɔ̃akɛ, eshopo tɛshɔshrɔ nɛ̀ ebela ebui nɛ ɔsha shu ɔzɔ wlɔ̃akɛ, ɔmɔsɛ kɛnanà ma ebui alɛ mɛ kɛlɛ alɛ shu axɔ̃ɛ waɔwɔa nanà tsyɛ ama. 10Kpɛ ɔsha ɔkɔ anɔ gɛ amana ɔtɔbubuɔ bɔkpɛ tunikpo, tɛ̀ owlui ɔkpɛ ɔsha ɔkɔɛ bɔbhalà. Nɛ kɛlɛ edieɛ, tɔɔkɔ fɔɛnii. 11Kɛlɛ sɛ otì ɔsɛ anɔ alɛ nɛidiɛ, yɛtakpumɛ ku otumũ kɛlɛ enu ebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ elete, ebe bu yɛishu ipɔ.
Buluhũ Huhu Gɔgɔɛ Nɔ
