He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever.
The Judgment at the Great White Throne
11 I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go. 12 I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
13The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done. 14Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death. 15Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
translation_text:
Agɔ Ohotu Zɛzɛɛ
1Ɛmɔ axɔ̃ɛdɛ teli gɛ ezhi ede ititíɛ, gɛ evunɔ ozĩ dzudzuɔ, ɔlɛ opupukɔ nɛ̀ ɔɖaohui gagalĩ toli. 2Evũ kɛtsɔpɛ, gɛinu ebeliɛ eblidzya, gɛenu abosamuɔ alo Satana, kɛlɛ anya yɛelu agɔ ohotu zɛzɛ̀. 3Awɔanyã yɛatɛ akpɛ ozĩ dzudzuɔ mɛ, adã ɔlɛ pɛ edere ɔlɛ ulũ akpii, sɛ tɛ atɛ mɔ idzo nanà tɛ ewlui azawɔɛ ɛmadiɛ, ɔvɛnɔ agɔ ohotu zɛzɛ̀ ede flogo. Nɛ agɔ alɛ niedeɛ, bebebui gɛ azabazã nɛ kibe gulũi mɛ.
4Ɛmɔ bagɛ bakɔ alɛidzo asɛ tɛ bekulu ubhuɔ bazã beekusi ugbu abha. Kɛlɛ ɛmɔ bagɛ alɛ atakpu bebhuiɛ atɔwuiɛ, bagɛ bazaɖɔaɖɔ adalɛ Yesu nɛ̀ Owusoso Akaa yɛibuiɛ shu soɔ. Bagɛ kpɛ ɛtakpu esi akɔ agãhoeɛ alo yɛafafatɛ, kɛlɛ bagɛ yɛkɛ yɛná tsatsà nɛ butúɔ alo ɛhɔɛ tsyɛ. Bazã nɛ̀ ɛkpã, kɛlɛ baŋã ukusi aplɛnɔ Kristo agɔ ohotu zɛzɛ̀. 5(Banɔ tsitsí gɔgɔ̃ɛ nɔ xɔ̃nɔ egi tsodzi anyɛmɛ ɔvɛnɔ kibeɛ gɛ agɔ ohotu zɛzɛ̀ ulũ bafala.) Kɛlinu tɛlɔlɔ́ odenɔ bekpuɔ mɛ gɛiputɔ. 6Tiyuyulà nɛ̀ tɛdadalɛ ɔkɔ bagɛ amɔ tɛlɔlɔ́ gɛiputɔ ɔlɛ. Butsí bhalà tɛbamɔ ohumi nɛ alɛ abha, pɔ bebèdzi Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Kristo balɛ anɔlũazazã, kɛlɛ babàŋã ukusi aplɛnii agɔ ohotu zɛzɛ̀.
Beté Satana
7Onupɛ agɔ ohotu zɛzɛ̀ ɔbɔfalaa, bebèbu Satana odenɔ yɛɔɖavũɔ mɛ 8kɛlɛ azabavɛ adɛwɔɛ ɛmadiɛ nɛ kesiakpa idekrekpu tɛlɛ nabha, gɛenu Gɔg nɛ̀ Magɔg tɛ edi shogò alɛ akɔ keyu tatá. Balɛ tikpekpè mɛ lɛ asɛ aƒutɔ̃ ayisɛ ana. 9Batsà ɛha afala nɛ kanɔ nɔ̀ mɛ, kɛlɛ bebu kɛma akpɛ Owusoso Akaa anɔ dadalɛ nɛ̀ ɔma hohoeɛ gɛ adɛmɛ nɔ. Pɔ kifù wɔlɛ ide kititíɛ pɛ kɛpɔɛ alɛ. 10Kɛlɛ balɔkɔ abosamuɔ gɛ awɔɛ alɛ, awɔanyã atɛ́ akpɛ aklagbuifuɔ gɛ ɛtã mɛ, nɛ ɛkɛlɛ gɛ batɛ́ɛ agãhoeɛ nɛ̀ asɛnɛ ebuiɖɔluanunɔ akpɛ. Babà hɔhɔlɛ alɛ elishi nɛ̀ emuze ɔvɛnɔ tihɔ, tihɔ.
Kebhu Gɔgɔɛ Kukulu Nɛ̀ Bekpuɔ
11Kɛlɛ ɛmɔ ekusiegbu hohoe fufú tieli, nɛ̀ anunɔ gɛ azã alɛ abhaa. Kesiakpa nɛ̀ kitití shɛ̃ nɛ yɛamɛ, kɛlɛ ɔzãkɔ nanà ɔgɛ ɔzã ɔkɔ alɛ. 12Kɛlɛ ɛmɔ banɔ tsitsí, besheshè nɛ̀ bunuvɔɛ gɛ bazã itití nɛ ekusiegbuɔ otugbà, kɛlɛ badalɛ uplukpa mɛ elu. Baza dalɛ eplukpa bhlatɔ tsyɛ gɛenu ɛkpã eplukpa. Bekulu ebhu nɛ̀ banɔ tsitsíɛ nɛ kɛzã gɛ bazã nɛ ɛkpã, pɛ bafɔnɔ elu nɛ buplukpa mɛ nulũ. 13Aƒu ebu bagɛ betsí nɛ yɛmɛ amana, kɛlɛ butsí nɛ̀ bekpuɔ ɔzãkɔ tsyɛ obu banɔ tsitsíɛ gɛ balɛ alɛ mɛɛ amana. Kɛlɛ bekulu ebhu akɔ anɔ nyia anɔ nɛ kɛgɛ yɛabhɛtɛ nulũ. 14Kɛlɛ balɔkɔ butsí nɛ̀ bekpuɔ ɔzãkɔ atɛ́ akpɛ kifu ɔɖɛklɛmɛ nɔ mɛ. Kifu ɔɖɛklɛmɛ nɔ ɔlɛ nu butsí bhalà. 15Onupɛ bágɛmɔ anɔ teli yɛinye sɛ bafɔnɔ ɛlɛ akpɛ ɛkpãeplukpa mɛɛ, balɔkɔ yɛatɛ́ akpɛ kifu ɔɖɛklɛmɛ nɔ mɛ.

english_text:
The New Heaven and the New Earth
1 I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea. 2 Then I saw New Jerusalem, the holy city, coming down from God in heaven. It was like a bride dressed in her wedding gown and ready to meet her husband.
3 I heard a loud voice shout from the throne:
God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people. 4 He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
5Then the one sitting on the throne said:
I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted. 6 Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty. 7 All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
8But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death.
The New Jerusalem
9I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.” 10 Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
11The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone. 12 The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel. 13Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west. 14The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
15 The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls. 16The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about 2,400 kilometers high and 2,400 kilometers wide. 17Then the angel measured the wall, and by our measurements it was about 60 meters high.
18 The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal. 19Each of the twelve foundations was a precious stone. The first was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was agate, the fourth was emerald, 20the fifth was onyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, the eighth was beryl, the ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst. 21Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
22I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple. 23 And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
24 Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there. 25 Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes. 26The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city. 27 But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
translation_text:
Kitití Vɔvɔ Nɛ̀ Kesiakpa Vɔvɔ
1Kidegbaa ɛmɔ itití vɔvɔ nɛ̀ esiakpa vɔvɔ. Ɔmɔsɛ kititíɛ gɛiputɔ nɛ̀ esiakpa gɛeputɔ ulũ tɛba fala, kɛlɛ aƒu nanà tsyɛ agabazã. 2Ɛmɔ Ɔma Dadalɛ gɛonu Yerusalem vɔvɔ loozhi ode Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ nɛititíɛ ɔba. Bewoese ɔlɛ piapii asɛ kiwoese gɛ bewoese adzɛ gɛ bakpɛ buluwɔ̃a akɔ anyɛ sɛ tɛ agbana ana. 3Kɛlɛ ilũ ozĩ loyi gagalĩ ode ekusiegbuɔ shu ɔsɛ, “Otsi alɛ Owusoso Akaa ɔzãkɔ lɛ banɔdzyrame ishi, kɛlɛ abazã aplɛnɔ alɛ. Bebedzi yɛanɔ, kɛlɛ Owusoso Akaa abazã balɛ ɔbha, pɛ yɛebedzi alɛ Owusoso Akaa. 4Ebetsuru ɛwɔ̃mɛ ka nyia ka nɛ alɛ amɛ. Butsí, ɔwɔ̃nyɔnɔ, yoyò bibi alo bɔtɔ nanà zɛbazã, ɔmɔsɛ ebui kukuɔ gɛazã ulũ tɛbafala.”
5Anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ azã ekusiegbu nabha aɖɔ asɛ, “Idzinɔ kɛka nyia ka bhɛtɛ ɛdɔ̃ vɔvɔ.!” Kɛlɛ azaɖɔ asɛ, “Fɔnɔ ɛlɛ olu, ɔmɔsɛ ebuiɛ alɛ àlɛ, alɛ ɔzãkɔ kɛlɛ lɛenu kɛgbã.”
6Etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Kɛka nyia ka na. Mɛinu anɔ gɛ eputɔ, pɛ ɛka wlui nu Agɔgɔɛ nɔ tsyɛ, mɛinu Kesikpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ Ɔtɔ̃kɔɔ. Anunɔ gɛ olũwɔɛ oyi yɛ ɛbakɔ yɛulĩ yɛawɔɛ odenɔ bulĩɛ gɛ bukoeyi odenɔ ɛkpã bulĩ ozĩɛ mɛ, gɛ atɛ babuia ɔlɛ ɛbhɛ. 7Anunɔ gɛ aŋã abhaa, bɔdɔ̃ ɛlɛ nyɔ̃ɔ badzi yɛɔyɛ, kɛlɛ ɛbadzi yɛ Owusoso Akaa, kɛlɛ yɛtsyɛ ebedzi mɛeyi. 8Pɔ kifusisíelete, bagɛ agɛyɛkɛ aŋã, atsɔ̃bui abhɛbhɛtɛelete, bagɛ beyì anɔ, aglɔŋaŋãelete, behuinɛ, ibudielete nɛ̀ asɛnɛ sɛsɛelete peteɛ xɔ̃nɔɔ-balɛ ɔzãkɔ obodzi kifu gɛtã nɛ̀ aklagbuifu ɔɖɛklɛmɛ nɔ mɛ. Kɛlinu butsi bhalàa.”
9Baxɔ̃ɛdɛ tegegene gɛ bevunɔ buyintse tugegene gɛ buyuku nɛ̀ bɔtɔ gɔgɔ̃ɛ nɔ gɛ bàba, ɔmayɛmɛ tenikpo aba kɛlɛ etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Ba, ɛbakasɛ wɔ adzɛ gɛ bakpɛ buluwɔ̃a eluɔ, gɛenu Obhoshieyiɛ yɛedzini.” 10Nɛ Ɔfɔfuĩa mɛɛ, efeke mɛ adɛ tɔɖɔ butu hohoe, ɖaɖama tuli abha, kɛlɛ ebu Ɔma Dadalɛ, gɛonu Yerusalem gɛode Owusoso Akaa ɔkɔ nɛititíɛ ozhiɛ akasɛ mɛ. 11Owusoso Akaa ishu dadalɛ zitse nɛ ɔlɛ mɛ, kɛlɛ ɔlɛ titsetseɛ zã ɔmɔ asɛ yaspa ɛklagbu, gɛ kɛlɛ mɛ dalɛ ɔmɔ asɛ ahuhɔ̃ɛ mɛ. 12Babhɔ egli dodó, ɖaɖama akana ɔmaa, gɛ budulũ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ tɔɔbha zã alɛishu. Baxɔ̃ɛdɛ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha azã budulũɔ. Nɛ budulũɔ shuɔ, bafɔnɔ Israel egbogbo lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha enye atɔɖɔ. 13Bebu budulũ tɔɔta nɛ kiwiɛ otukɔ nɛibe, tɔɔta lɛ piabhaɛbeɛ, tɔɔta lɛ pialɛ ibe kɛlɛ tɔɔta tsyɛ lɛ kiwiɛ ɔdɔkɔ nɛibe. 14Buvũ esikpɛkpɛ egli edi lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha babhɔ akɔ ɔma egiliɛ. Kɛlɛ nɛ kɛka nyia aka abhaa, bafɔnɔ Obhoshieyiɛ yɛanɔ kpɛkpɛ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha enyeɛ atɔɖɔ. 15Axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ aɖɔaɖɔ nɛ mɛɛ, evunɔ sikãishi tili idi gɛ alɔkɔ edzyidzyi ɔmaa, ɔlɛ budulũɔ nɛ̀ egiliɛ nɔ. 16Ɔma gɛ bebhuiɛ, ɔlɛ tidzedze mɛ nɛ̀ ɔlɛ tɛnɛnɛ̀ɛ mɛ pete ashɔ̃. Axɔ̃ɛdɛ edzyidzyi ɔma ɔlɛ kɛwɔã zɛ̀zɛ nɛ̀ ɔɖɛishì nɔ, kɛlɛ ɔlɛ tidzedzeɛ mɛ lɛ asɛ ɛtsɛkɔ ohotu zɛzɛ̀ nɛ̀ akpã talɛ̃. Ɔma ɔlɛ tidzedzeɛ, nɛ̀ tɛnɛnɛ̀ mɛ̀ pete shɔ̃. 17Axɔ̃ɛdɛ edzyidzyi egiliɛ tsyɛ kɛlɛ alɛ tɛɖaɖama mɛ zã asɛ bɔtsrɛkpɔ akpã taabha, kishi nɔ gɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ alɔkɔ nu anɔdzyrame ɔdɔ tidzyi dzyidzyí ɔyɛ. 18Balɔkɔ yaspa aklagbu abhɔ egiliɛ nɔ, kɛlɛ babhɔ ɔma nɛ̀ sikã gbangbã gɛ yɛmɛ adalɛ asɛ ahuhɔ̃ɛ ana. 19Balɔkɔ aklagbu gbangbã edi edi ewoese kesikpɛkpɛ egiliɛ nɔ. Kesikpɛkpɛ aklagbuɔ gɛeputɔ enu yaspa, tɛbhɔbhɔ tɔɖɔ ɔbhalà nu safir, atramɛ enu kalkedon, kɛlɛ alɛlɛmɛ enu emaragad, 20etrimiɛ enu sardonik, ehlomiɛ enu sardis, egegenemiɛ enu krisolit, asɛmiɛ enu beril, ezhitamiɛ enu topas, kɛfɔ̀miɛ nu krisopras, kɛfɔ̀ nɛ̀ toliɛ nu hiakink kɛlɛ kɛfɔ̀ nɛ̀ taabhaa nu ametist. 21Balɔkɔ bɔdɔ̃ gɛitse ŋelĩŋelĩɛ ewoese ɔma ɔlɛ budulũ lɔfɔ nɛ̀ tɔɔbhaa nɔ. Sikã gbangbã adɛ gɛ ɔbɔlà ɔmɔ bɔdɔ̃ ɔgana yɛmɛ asɛ ahuhɔ̃ɛ anaa, yɛ balɔkɔ akpɛ ɔma tipɔ hohoeɛ nɔ.
22Ɛgɛmɔ Owusoso Akaavũ nanà nɛ ɔmamɛ, Apalete Owusoso Akaa Ohumieleteɛ nɛ̀ Obhoshieyiɛ balɛ enu Owusoso Akaavũɔ nɛ ɛkɛlɛ. 23Kiwi nɛ̀ woele etienye nɛ ɔma mɛ, ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa tɛdadalɛ yɛakɔ ɔsɛ ɔlɛ mɛ ɔdalɛ, kɛlɛ Obhoshieyiɛ yɛenu ɔlɛ kifuɔ. 24Yɛtɛdadalɛ mɛ ɛmadiɛ abàgã, kɛlɛ kesiakpa abha ekusiɛ abalɔkɔ alɛ tɛpɔpɔɖiɛ mana ɔlɛ mɛ. 25Batɛ badã ɔma ɔlɛ budulũɔ eɖi, ɔmɔsɛ elishi nanà atɛ bazã ɛkɛlɛ. 26Ɛmadiɛ abalɔkɔ balɛishu dadalɛ nɛ̀ kinye kpɛkpɛ nɛitití amana ɔma ɔlɛ mɛ. 27Kɛdɔ̃ gɛ ɛlɛ shu gɛdalɛ nanà tɛbaku ɔma ɔlɛ mɛ ɖi, alo bagɛ babhɛtɛ awuĩ ebui alo asɛnɛ elete atɛ bazã ɔlɛ mɛ. Pɔ bagɛ alɛ enye bafɔnɔ akpɛ Obhoshieyiɛ ɛkpã eplukpa mɛ ete ko balɛ abazã ɛkɛlɛ.

english_text:
1 The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated. 2 Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
3 God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God 4 and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people. 5 Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever.
The Coming of Christ
6Then I was told:
These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon. 7Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
8My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
9But the angel said,
Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
10Don't keep the prophecies in this book a secret. These things will happen soon.
11 Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
12 Then I was told:
I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done. 13 I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
14 God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city. 15But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
16 I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
17 The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!”
Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!”
If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
18 Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book:
If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book. 19If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
20The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!”
So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
21I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
translation_text:
Ɛkpã Kelĩkekpuɔ
1Kɛlɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ akasɛ mɛ kelĩkekpuɔ gɛ kalɛ mɛ ulĩ kɔ ɛkpãa. Bulĩɛ mɛ dalɛ ɔmɔ asɛ ahuhɔ̃ɛ ana, kɛlɛ bɔkpɛ odenɔ Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Obhoshieyiɛ ekusiegbuɔ mɛ. 2Bɔkpɛ gã ɔma ɔlɛ tipɔ hohoe nɛishi tɛbha. Nɛ kelĩkekpuɔ ogbogbò ka nyia kaa, ɔbɔmɔ busiɛ gɛ akɔ ɛkpã tonikpo. Gɛ eyu eyuyu lɔfɔ nɛ̀ taabha tɔɖɔ nɛ woele ka nyia ka mɛ. Kɛlɛ busiɛ awɔlakpa enu bɔwa akɔ ɛmadiɛ. 3Kibui ɖɔɖɔ kpɛ anɔ nanà zaazã. Owusoso Akaa nɛ̀ Obhoshieyiɛ ekusiegbuɔ abazã ɔma mɛ kɛlɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ abaadɛ axɔ̃ɛ akɔɛ. 4Baba mɔ yɛamɛ kɛlɛ yɛinye bazã balɛ utukpe nɔ. 5Elishi azaazã. Ananà azapɛ kifu gɛkɔ tɛdadalɛ alo kiwi nyenye, ɔmɔsɛ Owusoso Akaa yɛabakɔ alɛ tɛdadalɛ. Kɛlɛ babà ŋã bukusi ɔvɛnɔ tihɔ, tihɔ. 6Axɔ̃ɛdɛ etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Ebui alɛ, lalɛ alɛ ɔzãkɔ kɛlɛ lɛenu ebui igbã. Apalete, Owusoso Akaa gɛenu ɛfɔfuĩa ɔkɔ ebuiɖɔluanɛ, yɛasɛsɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ asɛ tɛ akasɛ bagɛ adɛ yɛaxɔ̃ɛ ebui nɔnɔ gɛ ababà alɛ mɛ gulũi nɔ.”
Yesu Aaba
7“Noɖio ɛɛba otsiɛ tsĩɔɔ! Beyulà anunɔ gɛ anyana ebuiɛ gɛ baɖɔ elu nɛ eplukpa alɛ mɛ evunɔ.”
8Mɛ, Yohanes, mɛinu anunɔ gɛ ilũ pɛ ɛmɔ ebui alɛ. Kɛlɛ gɛ ilũ pɛ ɛmɔ alɛɛ, izhi kumu sɛ tɛ mɛkpɛ ɛtakpu esi nɛ axɔ̃ɛdɛ gɛ ezebu ebui alɛ akasɛ mɛ otugbà. 9Pɔ etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Tɛbhɛtɛ ana! Wɔhagbo axɔ̃ɛdɛ ete mɛinu ɔplɛni wɔ nɛ̀ wɔadzya alɛ ebuiɖɔluanɛ nɛ̀ bagɛ anyana ebuiɛ nɛ eplukpa alɛ mɛ evunɔ. Kpɛ ɛtakpu esi ɔkɔ Owusoso Akaa ete!”
10Kɛlɛ etseɖe mɛasɛ, “Tidere ɔdɔ̃ nɛ ebui alɛ gɛ baɖɔ elu nɛ eplukpa alɛ mɛ ulũ, ɔmɔsɛ kibeɛ tso ba. 11Nɔkɔ anunɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ ebui kpa azã yɛebui kpa bhɛbhɛtɛ abha, anunɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ atsɔ̃buiɛ, tɛ azã alɛ abha tɛ azabhɛtɛ, nɔkɔ anunɔ gɛ abhɛtɛ ebui gbangbã, tɛ azã alɛ abha tɛ azabhɛtɛ ebui gbangbã, kɛlɛ nɔkɔ anunɔ gɛ enu anɔ dadalɛ tsyɛ azã yɛ tɛdadalɛ nabha.”
12“Noɖio, ɛɛba otsiɛ tsĩɔɔ! Ivũnɔ kɛbhɛ gɛ ɛbabuia nɔ, kɛlɛ ɛbakɔ anɔ ka nyia ka nɛ kɛbhɛtɛ gɛ abhɛtɛ nulũ. 13Mɛinu anɔ Gɛeputɔ nɛ̀ Agɔgɔɛ nɔ, Agɛ alɛ Otugba nɛ̀ agɛ yɛalɛ Kedeɛ, Kesikpɛkpɛ nɛ̀ Ɔtɔ̃kɔ.
14Beyulà banunɔ gɛ beplu balɛ akpɛkpɛ dzloloɔ, sɛ tɛ bakɔ alɛ idzo nɛ busiɛ gɛ akɔ ɛkpãa shu, kɛlɛ tɛ bamɔ idzo agã afala nɛ budulũɔ tɛ betsoku ɔma mɛ. 15Bagɛ babàwɔ kevudeɛ, balɛ enu beebu, behuinɛ, bagɛ baaŋã agɔlɔ, bagɛ beyi anɔ, ibudielete nɛ̀ anɔ ka nyia ka gɛ adɛmɛ asɛnɛ sɛsɛ ebui pɛ asɛɔsɛ.
16Mɛinu Yesuɔ, gɛ ɛkpɛ mɛaxɔ̃ɛdɛ sɛ tɛ adalɛ ebui alɛ shu akɔ wɔ tɛ odi tseɖe bahameɛ. Mɛinu David yɛogu nɛ̀ Yɛeshui nɔ, nɛ̀ Kɛvɔlɔ Ɛwɔ̃ɛ gɛ kɔ tɛdadalɛɛ.”
17Ɔfɔfuĩa nɔ nɛ̀ anyɛedziniɛ aɖɔ asɛ, “Ba!” Anunɔ gɛ elũɔ aɖɔ asɛ, “Ba!” Anunɔ gɛ oluwɔ̃ɛ oyi yɛɛ, nɔkɔ yɛaba, kɛlɛ anunɔ gɛ apɛɛ nɔ, nɔkɔ yɛabawɔ̃ɛ bulĩɛ gɛkɔ ɛkpã faa, kɛbhɛ mabuia, mabuia.
18Itseɖe nɔ nɛ̀ anɔ nyia anɔ gɛ elũ ebui alɛ gɛ baɖɔ elu nɛ eplukpa alɛ mɛ asɛ, onupɛ anɔ teli alɔkɔ kibui tili aplɛnɔ alɛɛ, Owusoso Akaa abalɔkɔ bɔtɔ ebuiɛ gɛ baɖɔ nɛ eplukpa alɛ mɛɛ, aplɛnɔ akɔɛ. 19Kɛlɛ onupɛ anɔ teli ebu ebui teli tsyɛ eyinɔ nɛ ebuiɛ gɛ baɖɔ elu nɛ eplukpa alɛ mɛɛ, Owusoso Akaa tsyɛ abayɛkɛ anɔ alɛ yɛɔŋãkɔ gɛ ɔlɛ busiɛ gɛ akɔ ɛkpãa nɛ̀ ɔma dadalɛ ebuiɛ gɛ baɖɔ nɛ eplukpa alɛ mɛɛ, nɛ yɛahɔɛ.
20Anɔ nɔ̀ gɛ aɖɔaɖɔ adalɛ ebui alɛ shuɔ aɖɔ asɛ, “Ee, ɛɛba otsiɛ tsĩɔɔ.”
Amen, Apalete Yesu ba.
21Apalete Yesu tibhui bhuibu zã ɔplɛnɔ Owusoso Akaa yɛeyi alɛ oo. Amen.
